- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open composer and select the maximum supported time (e.g., 30 days) | Maximum time is selectable and displayed |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is delivered to sender and recipient |
| 3 | 3. Refresh chat before expiry | Message is still visible before the expiration time |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum supported time is accepted and message remains visible until expiration

---

### TC-148: Attempt to send with negative time value

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-018
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-008

**Description:** Ensure negative self-deletion times are rejected

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is reachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open composer and attempt to input a negative time value (e.g., -5 minutes) via UI or input field | System does not accept negative values or flags the input as invalid |
| 2 | 2. Click Send | Error is shown and message is not sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Negative self-deletion times are rejected and sending is blocked

---

### TC-149: Invalid format for self-deletion time

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-018
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-008

**Description:** Validate system behavior when time format is invalid or non-numeric

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is reachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open composer and enter a non-numeric or malformed time value (e.g., 'abc') if manual input is possible | Input is rejected or validation error appears |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to send the message | System prevents sending and shows an error |

**Final Expected Result:** Invalid time formats are not accepted and message is not sent

---

### TC-150: Auto-deletion after logout and re-login

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-018
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-008

**Description:** Ensure messages still auto-delete even if users log out and return after expiry

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a message with a short self-deletion time (e.g., 2 minutes) | Message is delivered to both users |
| 2 | 2. Both users log out before expiration | Users are logged out successfully |
| 3 | 3. Wait for the self-deletion time to elapse | Expiration time passes |
| 4 | 4. Both users log back in and open the conversation | Message is not visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Auto-deletion occurs regardless of login state at expiration

---

### TC-151: System performance: bulk messages with self-deletion

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-018
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-008

**Description:** Assess deletion processing under load for multiple messages

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is logged in
- Performance environment is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send 100 messages with self-deletion times within a short window (e.g., 1-5 minutes) | All messages are delivered successfully |
| 2 | 2. Monitor deletion execution and system resource usage during expiration window | Deletions occur within expected time and system remains stable |
| 3 | 3. Verify no expired messages remain visible after the window ends | All messages are removed from both users' views |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles bulk self-deleting messages without errors and deletes all on schedule

---

### TC-152: Send and open View-Once media exactly once

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Verifies that a view-once media is displayed exactly one time and then marked as non-retrievable

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient are registered users and logged in
- Recipient is reachable/online
- Sender has a valid media file selected and marked as view-once

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender sends the view-once media to the recipient | Message is delivered to recipient with view-once indicator |
| 2 | 2. Recipient opens the view-once media | Media is displayed successfully |
| 3 | 3. Recipient exits the media viewer | Media is marked as opened/consumed in the chat |

**Final Expected Result:** Media is displayed exactly once and then marked as not retrievable

---

### TC-153: Prevent re-opening of already viewed View-Once media

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures access is denied when attempting to re-open a previously viewed view-once media

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient has opened the view-once media once
- Media is marked as opened/consumed

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient attempts to open the same view-once media again | System blocks access to the media |
| 2 | 2. Observe the UI message | Hint/notification indicates the media can only be viewed once |

**Final Expected Result:** Re-opening is denied and a hint message is displayed

---

### TC-154: Prevent forwarding of already viewed View-Once media

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures forwarding is blocked after the media has been viewed once

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient has opened the view-once media once
- Media is marked as opened/consumed

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient attempts to forward the view-once media | Forward option is disabled or action is blocked |
| 2 | 2. Observe the UI message | Hint/notification indicates forwarding is not allowed |

**Final Expected Result:** Forwarding is denied and an informational hint is shown

---

### TC-155: Failed delivery does not mark media as opened

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Validates that failed delivery does not consume the view-once media

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is unreachable (e.g., offline or network failure)
- Sender has a valid media file selected and marked as view-once

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender sends the view-once media | Delivery attempt fails |
| 2 | 2. Observe sender notification | Error message indicates delivery failure |
| 3 | 3. Check message status on sender side | Media is not marked as opened/consumed |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives error and media remains unconsumed

---

### TC-156: Recipient opens view-once media while offline after delivery sync

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures the view-once behavior holds when recipient opens media after being offline

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient are registered users
- Recipient is offline at send time
- Sender has a valid media file selected and marked as view-once

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender sends view-once media while recipient is offline | Message shows as sent but pending delivery |
| 2 | 2. Recipient comes online and syncs messages | View-once media is delivered with indicator |
| 3 | 3. Recipient opens the media | Media is displayed successfully |
| 4 | 4. Recipient attempts to open again | Access is denied and a hint message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Media can be opened exactly once after sync

---

### TC-157: Multiple devices: view-once consumed on first device

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Verifies that viewing on one device consumes the media on all devices for the recipient

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient is logged in on two devices
- Sender has sent a view-once media
- Media is delivered to recipient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient opens the view-once media on Device A | Media is displayed successfully on Device A |
| 2 | 2. Recipient attempts to open the same media on Device B | Access is denied and a hint message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Media is consumed once across devices

---

### TC-158: Boundary: attempt to open view-once media immediately after send before fully loaded

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures proper handling when recipient opens media during initial loading state

**Preconditions:**
- Sender has sent a view-once media
- Recipient has slow network conditions
- Media is delivered but still loading

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient taps to open the media while loading | Loading indicator is shown |
| 2 | 2. Media finishes loading | Media is displayed successfully |
| 3 | 3. Recipient attempts to open again | Access is denied with a hint message |

**Final Expected Result:** Media is viewable once after loading completes

---

### TC-159: Negative: attempt to save or download view-once media

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures saving/downloading is blocked for view-once media

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient has received a view-once media
- Media has not yet been opened

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient attempts to save or download the view-once media without opening | Save/download action is blocked or not available |
| 2 | 2. Recipient opens the media and attempts to save/download | Save/download action is blocked or not available |

**Final Expected Result:** Saving or downloading view-once media is not permitted

---

### TC-160: Error handling: recipient cannot open due to corrupted media

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Validates that corrupted media does not get marked as consumed when it fails to open

**Preconditions:**
- Sender has sent a view-once media
- Media payload is corrupted or fails to decode on recipient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Recipient attempts to open the view-once media | Error message indicates media cannot be opened |
| 2 | 2. Recipient attempts to open again after error | Access is still allowed since media was not successfully viewed |

**Final Expected Result:** Failed open does not consume the view-once media

---

### TC-161: Resend after failed delivery

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-019
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-009

**Description:** Ensures resending after a failure delivers and consumes correctly

**Preconditions:**
- Initial send failed due to delivery error
- Sender has the option to retry sending

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender retries sending the view-once media | Message is delivered to recipient |
| 2 | 2. Recipient opens the media | Media is displayed successfully |
| 3 | 3. Recipient attempts to open again | Access is denied with a hint message |

**Final Expected Result:** Resent media behaves as view-once after successful delivery

---

### TC-162: TC01 - Chat sperren mit erfolgreicher Zusatz-Authentifizierung

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein geöffneter Chat nach erfolgreicher Zusatz-Authentifizierung gesperrt wird und nur nach erneuter Authentifizierung zugreifbar ist.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist geöffnet und entsperrt
- Zusätzliche Authentifizierung ist eingerichtet (z. B. PIN/Biometrie)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Wähle im geöffneten Chat die Option „Chat sperren“ | Dialog/Prompt zur zusätzlichen Authentifizierung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Führe die zusätzliche Authentifizierung erfolgreich aus | System bestätigt die erfolgreiche Authentifizierung |
| 3 | 3. Versuche den gleichen Chat erneut zu öffnen | Zugriff wird blockiert und eine neue Authentifizierungsabfrage erscheint |
| 4 | 4. Schließe die zusätzliche Authentifizierung erneut erfolgreich ab | Chat-Inhalt wird angezeigt und Zugriff ist möglich |

**Final Expected Result:** Chat ist nach dem Sperren nur mit erneuter erfolgreicher Zusatz-Authentifizierung zugreifbar.

---

### TC-163: TC02 - Chat sperren: Authentifizierung abgebrochen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Chat entsperrt bleibt, wenn der Nutzer die Zusatz-Authentifizierung abbricht.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist geöffnet und entsperrt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Wähle im geöffneten Chat die Option „Chat sperren“ | Dialog/Prompt zur zusätzlichen Authentifizierung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Brich die Authentifizierung ab (z. B. über Abbrechen/Zurück) | Fehlermeldung bzw. Hinweis zum Abbruch wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Versuche im gleichen Chat fortzufahren (z. B. Nachricht lesen) | Chat bleibt zugreifbar und entsperrt |

**Final Expected Result:** Chat bleibt entsperrt und eine klare Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt.

---

### TC-164: TC03 - Chat sperren: Authentifizierung fehlgeschlagen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Chat entsperrt bleibt, wenn die Zusatz-Authentifizierung fehlschlägt.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist geöffnet und entsperrt
- Zusätzliche Authentifizierung ist eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Wähle im geöffneten Chat die Option „Chat sperren“ | Dialog/Prompt zur zusätzlichen Authentifizierung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Gib falsche Authentifizierungsdaten ein | Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt und Authentifizierung ist fehlgeschlagen |
| 3 | 3. Versuche im gleichen Chat fortzufahren | Chat bleibt zugreifbar und entsperrt |

**Final Expected Result:** Chat bleibt entsperrt und eine klare Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt.

---

### TC-165: TC04 - Zugriff auf gesperrten Chat erfordert Authentifizierung

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass beim Öffnen eines gesperrten Chats zusätzliche Authentifizierung erforderlich ist.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist gesperrt
- Zusätzliche Authentifizierung ist eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche den gesperrten Chat zu öffnen | System fordert zusätzliche Authentifizierung an |
| 2 | 2. Gib gültige Authentifizierungsdaten ein | Authentifizierung ist erfolgreich |
| 3 | 3. Warte auf die Anzeige des Chats | Chat-Inhalt wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Zugriff auf den gesperrten Chat ist erst nach erfolgreicher Authentifizierung möglich.

---

### TC-166: TC05 - Zugriff auf gesperrten Chat mit wiederholtem Fehlversuch

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass bei fehlgeschlagener Authentifizierung der Zugriff weiter blockiert bleibt.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist gesperrt
- Zusätzliche Authentifizierung ist eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche den gesperrten Chat zu öffnen | System fordert zusätzliche Authentifizierung an |
| 2 | 2. Gib falsche Authentifizierungsdaten ein | Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Versuche den Chat erneut zu öffnen ohne erfolgreiche Authentifizierung | Zugriff bleibt blockiert und Authentifizierung wird erneut verlangt |

**Final Expected Result:** Ohne erfolgreiche Authentifizierung bleibt der gesperrte Chat unzugänglich.

---

### TC-167: TC06 - Mehrfaches Sperren eines bereits gesperrten Chats

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Prüft das Verhalten, wenn der Nutzer versucht, einen bereits gesperrten Chat erneut zu sperren.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist gesperrt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Chat-Optionen für den gesperrten Chat | Chat-Optionen werden angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Wähle die Option „Chat sperren“ erneut | System zeigt Hinweis an, dass der Chat bereits gesperrt ist oder fordert Authentifizierung ohne Statusänderung |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Chat bleibt gesperrt und das System verhält sich konsistent (kein Fehler/kein Entsperren).

---

### TC-168: TC07 - Abbruch der Authentifizierung beim Öffnen eines gesperrten Chats

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Zugriff blockiert bleibt, wenn die Authentifizierung beim Öffnen abgebrochen wird.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist gesperrt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche den gesperrten Chat zu öffnen | System fordert zusätzliche Authentifizierung an |
| 2 | 2. Brich die Authentifizierung ab | Abbruchmeldung wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Versuche erneut, den Chat zu öffnen | Authentifizierungsabfrage erscheint erneut und Zugriff bleibt blockiert |

**Final Expected Result:** Der gesperrte Chat bleibt unzugänglich bis zur erfolgreichen Authentifizierung.

---

### TC-169: TC08 - Sperrstatus bleibt nach App-Neustart bestehen

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert Persistenz der Sperre über App-Neustart hinweg.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist gesperrt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Beende die Anwendung vollständig | App ist geschlossen |
| 2 | 2. Starte die Anwendung neu und öffne die Chatliste | Chatliste wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Versuche den gesperrten Chat zu öffnen | Authentifizierungsabfrage erscheint und Zugriff ist blockiert |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Sperrstatus bleibt nach Neustart erhalten und erfordert Authentifizierung.

---

### TC-170: TC09 - Sperrung nur für ausgewählten Chat (Boundary: mehrere Chats)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass nur der ausgewählte Chat gesperrt wird, andere Chats bleiben zugreifbar.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Mindestens zwei Chats existieren und sind entsperrt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne Chat A und wähle „Chat sperren“ | Authentifizierungsabfrage erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Authentifiziere dich erfolgreich | Chat A wird gesperrt |
| 3 | 3. Öffne Chat B | Chat B ist ohne zusätzliche Authentifizierung zugreifbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Nur der gesperrte Chat erfordert zusätzliche Authentifizierung; andere Chats bleiben entsperrt.

---

### TC-171: TC10 - Fehlermeldungstext bei Authentifizierungsfehler ist klar

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-020
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-010

**Description:** Prüft die Qualität der Fehlermeldung bei fehlgeschlagener Authentifizierung.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist registriert und angemeldet
- Ein Chat ist geöffnet und entsperrt
- Zusätzliche Authentifizierung ist eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Wähle „Chat sperren“ und gib falsche Authentifizierungsdaten ein | Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Fehlermeldung ist klar, verständlich und weist auf den Authentifizierungsfehler hin.

---

### TC-172: Send broadcast message to valid list with multiple recipients

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify successful delivery and status display when sending to a valid broadcast list.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with multiple valid recipients
- System messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Broadcast Lists page | Broadcast Lists page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the valid broadcast list with multiple recipients | List details including recipient count are shown |
| 3 | 3. Compose a message and click Send | Send action is initiated and a sending status indicator appears |
| 4 | 4. Observe delivery status updates | Delivery status for all recipients is shown as delivered or pending until complete |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is delivered to all recipients and the final delivery status is displayed.

---

### TC-173: Prevent sending when broadcast list is empty

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify sending is blocked with a clear error for an empty list.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with zero recipients

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the empty broadcast list | List details show zero recipients |
| 2 | 2. Enter a message and click Send | Send action is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Review the error message | A clear error indicates the list has no valid recipients |

**Final Expected Result:** Sending is prevented and a user-friendly error is displayed.

---

### TC-174: Prevent sending when broadcast list has only invalid recipients

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify sending is blocked if all recipients are invalid.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with recipients marked invalid (e.g., malformed numbers)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast list containing invalid recipients | List details show recipients with invalid status |
| 2 | 2. Enter a message and click Send | Send action is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Review the error message | A clear error indicates there are no valid recipients |

**Final Expected Result:** Sending is prevented and an error message is shown.

---

### TC-175: Handle mixed valid and invalid recipients

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify the system behavior when the list includes both valid and invalid recipients.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with a mix of valid and invalid recipients

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the mixed broadcast list | List details show both valid and invalid recipients |
| 2 | 2. Enter a message and click Send | Send action proceeds for valid recipients |
| 3 | 3. View delivery status | Valid recipients show delivery status; invalid recipients are flagged as failed/invalid |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is sent to valid recipients, and invalid recipients are reported appropriately.

---

### TC-176: Rate-limit exceeds configured threshold

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify the system delays or blocks sending and informs the user when recipient count exceeds the rate limit.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- Rate-limit is configured (e.g., max recipients per minute)
- A broadcast list exceeds the configured limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast list exceeding the rate limit | List details show recipient count above the limit |
| 2 | 2. Enter a message and click Send | System applies rate-limiting behavior |
| 3 | 3. Observe user notification | User is informed that sending is delayed or blocked due to rate limits |

**Final Expected Result:** Sending is delayed or blocked based on rate-limit rules and the user is informed.

---

### TC-177: Rate-limit boundary condition at exact threshold

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify sending succeeds when recipient count equals the configured rate limit.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- Rate-limit is configured
- A broadcast list has recipients exactly equal to the rate-limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast list at the rate-limit threshold | List details show recipient count equals the limit |
| 2 | 2. Enter a message and click Send | Send action is initiated without rate-limit block |
| 3 | 3. Observe delivery status | Delivery status updates for all recipients |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is sent successfully and status is displayed.

---

### TC-178: Display of sending status for each recipient

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify that the UI shows sending status updates reliably.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with multiple valid recipients

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast list | List details display recipients |
| 2 | 2. Send a message | Sending status is shown (e.g., queued/sent/delivered/failed) |
| 3 | 3. Wait for completion | Final status for all recipients is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** UI consistently displays per-recipient status until completion.

---

### TC-179: Unauthorized user cannot send broadcast

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify access control prevents unauthorized users from sending broadcast messages.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in without permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with valid recipients

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to open the broadcast send screen | Access is denied or send controls are disabled |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to send a message (if possible) | Send action is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Review the error or warning message | User is informed about insufficient permissions |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthorized user cannot send broadcast messages.

---

### TC-180: Message send with network failure during delivery

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify system handles transient delivery failures and status reflects failures.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with multiple valid recipients
- Messaging service is configured to simulate partial delivery failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a broadcast message | Sending status indicator appears |
| 2 | 2. Simulate network failure for a subset of recipients | Statuses for affected recipients show failed or retry |
| 3 | 3. Observe final status after retries (if applicable) | Final status reflects delivered or failed per recipient |

**Final Expected Result:** Failures are accurately reported and do not block delivery to other recipients.

---

### TC-181: Performance under large broadcast list within limits

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-021
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-011

**Description:** Verify sending and status update performance for large valid lists within configured limits.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with permission to send broadcasts
- A broadcast list exists with a large number of valid recipients within rate limits
- System performance monitoring is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the large broadcast list | List details load within acceptable time |
| 2 | 2. Send a broadcast message | Send action starts without UI freeze |
| 3 | 3. Monitor status updates | Status updates progress smoothly and complete within expected SLA |

**Final Expected Result:** System sends message efficiently and displays status within performance targets.

---

### TC-182: Send message with bold, italic, and bullet list formatting

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify supported formatting renders correctly for recipient

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor
- Recipient account exists and can receive messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open Nachrichten-Editor and enter text with bold, italic, and bullet list formatting | Text appears in editor with bold, italic, and bullet list styles applied |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is sent successfully and appears in Sent items |
| 3 | 3. Open the message as the recipient | Message displays with correct bold, italic, and bullet list formatting |

**Final Expected Result:** Recipient sees the message with correct supported formatting

---

### TC-183: Remove formatting before sending

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify formatting removal is respected on send

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter text and apply bold and italic formatting to a selection | Selected text is displayed as bold and italic in the editor |
| 2 | 2. Remove formatting from the selected text | Selected text returns to plain style in the editor |
| 3 | 3. Save or send the message | Message is saved/sent without errors |
| 4 | 4. Open the saved/sent message | Text is displayed without bold or italic formatting |

**Final Expected Result:** Removed formatting does not appear in the saved or sent message

---

### TC-184: Unsupported formatting is stripped on send

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify unsupported formatting is removed or converted to plain text

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Paste text with unsupported formatting (e.g., underline, font color, or HTML tags) | Pasted text appears in the editor (unsupported styles may show or be flattened) |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is sent successfully without validation errors |
| 3 | 3. Open the message as the recipient | Unsupported formatting is removed or converted to plain text; supported formatting remains |

**Final Expected Result:** Unsupported formatting does not block sending and is removed or plain-texted

---

### TC-185: Mixed supported and unsupported formatting

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Ensure supported formatting is preserved while unsupported is removed

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a paragraph with bold and italic, then apply unsupported formatting (e.g., highlight) to another part | Supported formatting appears; unsupported formatting may appear in editor |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message sends successfully |
| 3 | 3. View the message as recipient | Bold and italic remain; unsupported formatting is absent or plain text |

**Final Expected Result:** Supported formatting is retained and unsupported formatting is removed

---

### TC-186: Bullet list formatting with nested items

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify list formatting is preserved for nested bullet lists

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a bullet list with at least one nested sub-item | Editor displays a nested bullet list correctly |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message sends successfully |
| 3 | 3. Open the message as recipient | Nested bullet list formatting is preserved |

**Final Expected Result:** Nested bullet list displays correctly for the recipient

---

### TC-187: Formatting boundary on empty selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Ensure applying formatting with no selection behaves correctly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Place cursor in editor without selecting text | Cursor is visible with no text selected |
| 2 | 2. Click bold and then type text | Newly typed text appears bold in editor |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message sends successfully |
| 4 | 4. Open the message as recipient | Typed text appears in bold |

**Final Expected Result:** Formatting toggles apply to newly typed text and are preserved

---

### TC-188: Remove formatting from partial selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify only selected portion loses formatting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a sentence and apply bold to the entire sentence | Entire sentence appears bold |
| 2 | 2. Select a subset of the sentence and remove bold formatting | Only the selected portion returns to normal style |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message sends successfully |
| 4 | 4. View the message as recipient | Only the unformatted subset appears normal; the rest remains bold |

**Final Expected Result:** Formatting removal affects only the selected text

---

### TC-189: Paste plain text into formatted section

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Ensure pasted plain text does not inherit unsupported formatting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create italic text and place cursor within it | Cursor is within italic formatted text |
| 2 | 2. Paste plain text | Pasted text inherits italic formatting only if supported by editor behavior |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message sends successfully |
| 4 | 4. View as recipient | Pasted text displays with supported formatting only |

**Final Expected Result:** Pasted text displays with supported formatting and no unsupported styles

---

### TC-190: Unsupported formatting does not block sending

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Negative test to confirm sending is not prevented by unsupported formatting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Paste text containing complex HTML (e.g., tables, divs) | Text is inserted into the editor |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message sends successfully without errors or blocks |
| 3 | 3. View the message as recipient | Complex HTML is stripped or shown as plain text |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is delivered and unsupported HTML is removed or plain-texted

---

### TC-191: Save draft retains supported formatting

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Verify supported formatting persists when saving draft

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter text with bold, italic, and bullet list formatting | Formatting is visible in editor |
| 2 | 2. Save the message as draft | Draft is saved successfully |
| 3 | 3. Reopen the draft | Formatting is preserved in the draft |

**Final Expected Result:** Supported formatting persists across draft save/reopen

---

### TC-192: Large message with multiple formatted sections

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-022
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-012

**Description:** Boundary test for long messages with formatting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- User has access to Nachrichten-Editor

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a long message near the maximum allowed length with multiple formatted sections and lists | Editor remains responsive and formatting is visible |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message sends successfully within acceptable time |
| 3 | 3. View message as recipient | All supported formatting is correctly rendered |

**Final Expected Result:** Long formatted messages send and render correctly

---

### TC-193: Single @mention from participant list highlights mentioned user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that selecting a valid participant after typing @ sends message and highlights mention

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 3 participants
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' in the message input | Participant suggestion list appears |
| 3 | 3. Select a valid participant from the suggestion list | Selected participant appears as a mention token in the input |
| 4 | 4. Type additional text and send the message | Message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is delivered to the group and the mentioned participant is highlighted as an @mention

---

### TC-194: Multiple @mentions in one message are all highlighted

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that multiple valid mentions in a single message are correctly marked and delivered

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 4 participants

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' and select Participant A from the suggestion list | Participant A appears as a mention token in the input |
| 3 | 3. Add a space, type '@' and select Participant B from the suggestion list | Participant B appears as a mention token in the input |
| 4 | 4. Send the message | Message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is delivered to all group members and both mentioned participants are highlighted

---

### TC-195: Invalid @mention not in group is rejected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that mentions of non-members are prevented or removed with an error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 3 participants

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@NonMemberName' manually without selecting from the list | No valid mention token is created |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send the message | System prevents sending or removes the invalid mention and shows an error notification |

**Final Expected Result:** Invalid mention is not allowed and user is informed of the error

---

### TC-196: Mention suggestion list filters participants by typed characters

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that the mention list filters as the user types after @

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 5 participants
- At least two participants share similar name prefixes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@an' in the message input | Suggestion list appears filtered to participants with names starting with 'an' |
| 3 | 3. Select one of the filtered participants | Selected participant appears as a mention token in the input |

**Final Expected Result:** Mention list filters correctly and selected participant is inserted as a mention

---

### TC-197: Sending message with '@' only should not create mention

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that a lone '@' does not create an invalid mention

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 2 participants

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' and add regular text without selecting a participant | No mention token is created |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message is sent as plain text or system prompts to select a participant |

**Final Expected Result:** No invalid mention is created; message handling matches product rules

---

### TC-198: Mention of participant with special characters in name

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that mentions work with names containing special characters (e.g., umlauts)

- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with a participant named 'Jörg' or similar

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' and select the participant with special characters from the list | Mention token with the participant name is inserted |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Mention is highlighted correctly and delivered to all participants

---

### TC-199: Duplicate mentions of same participant in one message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that multiple mentions of the same participant are handled correctly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 3 participants

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Add the same participant twice using @mention selection | Two mention tokens for the same participant appear in the input |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Both mentions are highlighted and message delivery is successful

### TC-200: Mention list does not include non-members

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that suggestion list only shows current group members

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with known members
- A known non-member exists in the system

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' and scroll through the suggestion list | Only group members appear in the list |

**Final Expected Result:** Suggestion list contains only current group members

---

### TC-201: E2E: Mentioned user receives highlighted mention

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify end-to-end that the mentioned participant sees the highlight

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B are logged in on separate devices/sessions
- Both users are in the same group chat

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A opens the group chat and mentions User B using @ selection | User A sees the mention token in the message input |
| 2 | 2. User A sends the message | Message is delivered in the chat for User A |
| 3 | 3. User B views the new message | User B sees the message with the mention highlighted |

**Final Expected Result:** Mentioned user receives the message with highlighted @mention

### TC-202: Performance: Suggestion list appears promptly after typing '@'

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-023
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-013

**Description:** Verify that the mention suggestion list appears within acceptable time

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in a group chat with at least 50 participants
- Performance monitoring is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the group chat conversation | Group chat is displayed with message input field |
| 2 | 2. Type '@' in the message input | Suggestion list appears within 300ms |

**Final Expected Result:** Suggestion list loads within the defined performance threshold

---

### TC-203: Share current location successfully with permission

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that a user with granted location permission can share current location and recipient receives map preview and coordinates

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted at OS and app level
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat options menu | Chat options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Standort teilen" (Share location) option | Location sharing UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm sending the current location | Location message is sent and appears in the chat |
| 4 | 4. Verify the message content on sender side | Message shows a map preview and coordinates |
| 5 | 5. Verify the message content on recipient side | Recipient receives a location message with map preview and coordinates |

**Final Expected Result:** Location is shared as a message with map preview and coordinates to the recipient

---

### TC-204: Share selected location successfully with permission

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that a user can select and share a custom location and recipient receives map preview and coordinates

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted at OS and app level
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat options menu | Chat options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Standort teilen" (Share location) option | Location sharing UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Choose a different location on the map and confirm | Selected location is confirmed for sending |
| 4 | 4. Send the selected location | Location message is sent and appears in the chat |
| 5 | 5. Verify the message content on recipient side | Recipient receives a location message with map preview and coordinates of the selected location |

**Final Expected Result:** Selected location is shared as a message with map preview and coordinates to the recipient

---

### TC-205: Location permission denied by user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that location is not shared when user denies permission and a clear prompt is shown

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is not granted at OS or app level
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat options menu | Chat options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Standort teilen" (Share location) option | Permission request dialog is shown |
| 3 | 3. Deny the location permission request | Location is not shared |
| 4 | 4. Observe the UI prompt | A clear and understandable prompt to enable location permission is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Location is not shared and user receives a clear permission prompt

---

### TC-206: Location permission previously denied with "Don't ask again"

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that a clear prompt is shown and location is not shared when permission is permanently denied

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is permanently denied at OS level
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat options menu | Chat options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Standort teilen" (Share location) option | App displays permission guidance without requesting OS permission |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to proceed with sending location | Location is not shared |

**Final Expected Result:** Location is not shared and a clear guidance to enable permission in settings is displayed

---

### TC-207: Network instability during location send

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify error handling and retry option when network is unstable

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted
- Network connection is unstable or intermittently unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat options menu | Chat options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Standort teilen" option | Location sharing UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send current location | Send fails and an error message is displayed |
| 4 | 4. Tap the retry option | System attempts to resend the location |

**Final Expected Result:** An error message with retry option is shown and resend is attempted

---

### TC-208: Retry succeeds after temporary network recovery

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that retry sends location successfully once network stabilizes

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted
- Network connection can be restored after a failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate location sharing and simulate a network failure | Send fails and an error message with retry is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Restore a stable network connection | Network status becomes stable |
| 3 | 3. Tap the retry option | Location message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Location is successfully sent after retry when network stabilizes

---

### TC-209: Attempt to share location without open chat

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify location sharing is only available within an open chat context

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- No chat is currently open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the app main screen without opening a chat | Main screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Look for the "Standort teilen" option | Location sharing option is not available |

**Final Expected Result:** Location sharing is not available outside an open chat

---

### TC-210: Boundary: Rapidly tap send location multiple times

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify that repeated taps do not create duplicate location messages

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the location sharing UI | Location sharing UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Rapidly tap the send button multiple times | System processes a single send request or de-duplicates requests |
| 3 | 3. Check the chat history | Only one location message is present |

**Final Expected Result:** No duplicate location messages are sent

---

### TC-211: Boundary: Location retrieval timeout

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Verify behavior when location retrieval times out

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted
- GPS/location services are slow or unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select "Standort teilen" to get current location | Location retrieval starts |
| 2 | 2. Wait until the location request times out | A timeout error or guidance message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Location is not shared and user is informed about the timeout with guidance to retry

---

### TC-212: Integration: Verify coordinates format and map preview rendering

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-024
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-014

**Description:** Validate that the sent location includes valid coordinates and renders a map preview

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a registered user
- Chat with another user is open
- Location permission is granted
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send current location in an open chat | Location message appears in chat |
| 2 | 2. Inspect the message payload/metadata (via logs or debug tools) | Coordinates are present and in valid latitude/longitude format |
| 3 | 3. Verify the map preview is rendered in the message | Map preview is visible and corresponds to the coordinates |

**Final Expected Result:** Message contains valid coordinates and a correctly rendered map preview

---

### TC-213: Share contact successfully in an existing chat

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Verify that a selected contact is shared as a message and delivered to the recipient

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat with a recipient
- User has at least one contact in address book
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat where the contact will be shared | Chat thread is displayed and ready for input |
| 2 | 2. Click the "Kontakt teilen" (Share Contact) action | Contact selection UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Select a contact from the list | Selected contact is highlighted or shown as chosen |
| 4 | 4. Confirm sharing the selected contact | A contact card/message appears in the chat as the latest message |
| 5 | 5. Verify on the recipient side (or via delivery status) that the message was delivered | Recipient receives the contact message or delivery status is marked as delivered |

**Final Expected Result:** Selected contact is posted in the chat and delivered to the recipient

---

### TC-214: Cancel share contact flow

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Verify that canceling contact selection does not alter the chat

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- User has at least one contact in address book

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat where the contact could be shared | Chat thread is displayed and ready for input |
| 2 | 2. Click the "Kontakt teilen" (Share Contact) action | Contact selection UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Click "Cancel" or close the contact selection UI | Contact selection UI closes without confirmation |
| 4 | 4. Review the chat thread | No new message is added and the chat remains unchanged |

**Final Expected Result:** No contact is shared and the chat remains unchanged

---

### TC-215: Share contact with no network connection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Verify error handling when attempting to share a contact without network connectivity

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- User has at least one contact in address book
- Network connection is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat where the contact will be shared | Chat thread is displayed and ready for input |
| 2 | 2. Click the "Kontakt teilen" (Share Contact) action | Contact selection UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Select a contact from the list | Selected contact is highlighted or shown as chosen |
| 4 | 4. Confirm sharing the selected contact | An error message is shown indicating network issue |
| 5 | 5. Review the chat thread | No contact message is posted in the chat |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees a network error and no contact is sent

---

### TC-216: Share contact when contact list is empty

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Verify that the UI handles empty contacts list gracefully

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- User has no contacts in address book
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat | Chat thread is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the "Kontakt teilen" (Share Contact) action | Contact selection UI is displayed with an empty state message |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to proceed without a contact | Sharing action is disabled or a validation message indicates no contacts available |

**Final Expected Result:** User cannot share a contact and sees an appropriate empty state or validation message

---

### TC-217: Share contact when access to contacts is denied

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Verify proper handling when contact permission is not granted

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Contact permission is denied by the OS or app settings
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat | Chat thread is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the "Kontakt teilen" (Share Contact) action | A permission prompt or error is displayed indicating access is required |
| 3 | 3. Dismiss the prompt without granting permission | Contact selection UI is not opened and no message is added |

**Final Expected Result:** User is informed about missing permission and no contact is shared

### TC-218: Verify contact message content format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-025
**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

**Description:** Ensure that the contact message contains expected fields such as name and phone/email

- User is in an existing chat
- User has a contact with name and phone/email
- Network connection is available

|---|--------|------------------|

| 2 | 2. Click "Kontakt teilen" and select a contact with name and phone/email | Selected contact is shown as chosen |
| 3 | 3. Confirm sharing the contact | Contact message appears in chat |
| 4 | 4. Open the contact message details | Contact message displays the correct name and at least one contact detail (phone/email) |

**Final Expected Result:** Contact message content is correctly formatted and complete

### TC-219: Share contact with minimal details (boundary case)

**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual

**Requirement:** WA-MSG-015

### TC-220: Create group chat with valid name and at least two contacts

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify group creation succeeds with valid inputs and user becomes admin

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least three contacts are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tap on 'Neue Gruppe' or group creation entry point | Group creation screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid group name (e.g., 'Projekt Alpha') | Group name field shows the entered text |
| 3 | 3. Select two contacts from the list | Two contacts are marked as selected and selection count updates |
| 4 | 4. Tap on 'Gruppe erstellen' | Group creation request is submitted |

**Final Expected Result:** Group chat is created, the user is set as group admin, and the new chat appears in the chat list

---

### TC-221: Prevent group creation when fewer than two contacts selected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify validation blocks creation and shows hint when less than two contacts are selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least one contact is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open group creation screen | Group creation screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid group name | Group name field shows the entered text |
| 3 | 3. Select only one contact | One contact is marked as selected |
| 4 | 4. Tap on 'Gruppe erstellen' | Creation is blocked and a hint message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Group chat is not created and a clear message indicates at least two contacts are required

---

### TC-222: Prevent group creation when no contacts selected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify validation blocks creation and shows hint when zero contacts are selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least one contact is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open group creation screen | Group creation screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid group name | Group name field shows the entered text |
| 3 | 3. Do not select any contacts | No contacts are marked as selected |
| 4 | 4. Tap on 'Gruppe erstellen' | Creation is blocked and a hint message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Group chat is not created and a clear message indicates at least two contacts are required

---

### TC-223: Group creation with exactly two contacts (boundary condition)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify group creation succeeds at minimum valid selection count

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least two contacts are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open group creation screen | Group creation screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid group name | Group name field shows the entered text |
| 3 | 3. Select exactly two contacts | Two contacts are marked as selected |
| 4 | 4. Tap on 'Gruppe erstellen' | Group creation request is submitted |

**Final Expected Result:** Group chat is created successfully and appears in the chat list
