### TC-224: Notify user when invitations fail but group is created

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify group is created even if message delivery to one or more participants fails, and user is informed

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least three contacts are available
- One selected contact has simulated delivery failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open group creation screen | Group creation screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid group name | Group name field shows the entered text |
| 3 | 3. Select three contacts including one with delivery failure | Three contacts are marked as selected |
| 4 | 4. Tap on 'Gruppe erstellen' | Group creation request is submitted |
| 5 | 5. Observe notification after creation | User is informed that one or more invitations failed |

**Final Expected Result:** Group chat is created and visible, with a status hint indicating failed invitations

---

### TC-225: Group appears in chat list after successful creation

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify newly created group is displayed in the chat overview list

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least two contacts are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a new group with valid name and two contacts | Group creation succeeds |
| 2 | 2. Return to chat overview (if not automatically returned) | Chat overview is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Search or scroll for the newly created group name | New group appears in the chat list |

**Final Expected Result:** Newly created group chat is visible in the chat list with correct name

---

### TC-226: User becomes group admin after creation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-026
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-001

**Description:** Verify creator is assigned admin role in the new group

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on chat overview
- At least two contacts are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a new group with valid name and two contacts | Group creation succeeds |
| 2 | 2. Open the newly created group chat | Group chat view is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Open group info or participants list | Group info is displayed with participant roles |

**Final Expected Result:** The creator is marked as group admin in group info

---

### TC-227: Add member to group and audit log update

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify that adding a member by a group admin updates the UI immediately and logs the change

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Existing group with administrator rights
- Audit/protocol log is enabled for the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Group administration page is displayed with member management section |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Add member' and select a valid user | Selected user appears in the add member form |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the addition | Member appears immediately in the member list with default role |
| 4 | 4. Open the group protocol/audit log | Log entry shows the member addition with timestamp and admin identity |

**Final Expected Result:** Member is added, changes are visible immediately, and the action is logged

---

### TC-228: Remove member from group and audit log update

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify that removing a member updates UI immediately and logs the change

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Existing group with at least one removable member
- Audit/protocol log is enabled for the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an existing member and click 'Remove' | Confirmation dialog appears |
| 3 | 3. Confirm removal | Member is removed from list immediately |
| 4 | 4. Open the group protocol/audit log | Log entry shows the member removal with timestamp and admin identity |

**Final Expected Result:** Member is removed, change is visible immediately, and the action is logged

---

### TC-229: Change member role and audit log update

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify role change takes effect immediately and is logged

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Existing group with at least one member whose role can be changed
- Audit/protocol log is enabled for the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member list with roles is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a member and open role change options | Available roles are shown |
| 3 | 3. Assign a valid new role and save | Role change is reflected immediately in the member list |
| 4 | 4. Open the group protocol/audit log | Log entry shows the role change with old/new roles, timestamp, and admin identity |

**Final Expected Result:** Role change is applied immediately and recorded in the protocol

---

### TC-230: Block role assignment that violates privacy/security rules

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify system blocks prohibited role assignment and shows clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Group has defined privacy/security rules restricting certain role assignments
- Member exists for role update

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to assign a role that violates the group's rules | System validates the requested role assignment |
| 3 | 3. Submit the role change | Action is blocked and a clear, understandable error message is shown |
| 4 | 4. Check the member list | Member retains original role with no changes applied |

**Final Expected Result:** Prohibited role assignment is blocked with a clear error message

---

### TC-231: Search member in a large group and update role with no delay

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify performant search and immediate confirmation of role update in a large group

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Group has a large number of members (e.g., 10,000+)
- Target member exists with known name/identifier

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member management section is loaded |
| 2 | 2. Use search to find the target member by name or identifier | Search results return quickly and display the target member |
| 3 | 3. Update the member's role to a valid role | System confirms the role change without noticeable delay |
| 4 | 4. Verify role display in the results list | Updated role is shown immediately |

**Final Expected Result:** Search is performant and role update is confirmed and displayed without delay

---

### TC-232: Attempt role assignment by non-admin user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify access control prevents non-admin users from managing members

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a non-admin group member
- Existing group where user lacks administrator rights

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration or member management page | Access is denied or admin controls are hidden/disabled |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to change a member role via direct URL or UI manipulation | System blocks the action and shows an authorization error |

**Final Expected Result:** Non-admin user cannot manage members or change roles

---

### TC-233: Boundary: Add member with maximum allowed group size reached

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify system behavior when adding a member at the group size limit

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Group has reached its maximum allowed member count
- System enforces group size limits

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member management section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to add a new member | System validates group size constraints |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the addition | Action is blocked with a clear message indicating the limit |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents adding members beyond the maximum size with a clear error message

---

### TC-234: Boundary: Search with empty or minimal input in large group

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify search behavior when input is empty or too short and ensure no performance issues

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Group has a large number of members

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member management section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter an empty string or a single character in the search field | System handles input gracefully and provides guidance or default results |
| 3 | 3. Submit the search | Results return without delay and no errors are shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Search handles minimal input gracefully and remains performant

---

### TC-235: Audit log entry for role change includes required details

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify audit log records necessary details after a role update

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Audit log system is integrated and enabled
- Member exists for role update

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Change a member role to a valid new role | Role change is applied successfully |
| 2 | 2. Open the audit/protocol log | Log entries are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Locate the log entry for the role change | Entry contains member identifier, old role, new role, timestamp, and admin user ID |

**Final Expected Result:** Audit log entry is complete and accurate for role changes

---

### TC-236: Immediate UI update after member removal without page refresh

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-027
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-002

**Description:** Verify UI updates instantly after removal without requiring page reload

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Existing group with at least one removable member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group's administration page | Member list is visible |
| 2 | 2. Remove a member and confirm | Member disappears from the list immediately without page reload |
| 3 | 3. Verify no manual refresh has occurred | User remains on the same page with updated list |

**Final Expected Result:** Member removal is reflected immediately in the UI without refresh

---

### TC-237: Save valid group settings and apply immediately

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Verify that allowed settings can be changed, saved, and applied immediately by a group admin.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User has Gruppenadministrator role for the group
- User is on Gruppenverwaltung > Gruppeneinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Update a permitted setting (e.g., group visibility) to a new valid value | The new value is shown in the form field |
| 2 | 2. Update another permitted setting (e.g., posting permissions) to a valid value | The new value is shown in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Click on the save button | A success confirmation message is displayed |
| 4 | 4. Refresh or re-open the Gruppeneinstellungen page | Previously saved values are persisted and displayed |
| 5 | 5. Verify immediate application by checking a dependent behavior (e.g., visibility or posting permissions for a member) | Group behavior reflects the new settings without delay |

**Final Expected Result:** Valid settings are saved, persisted, and immediately applied.

---

### TC-238: Prevent saving when required fields are empty

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Ensure the system blocks saving and shows errors when required fields are left empty.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Clear a required field (e.g., group name or required policy field) | The field appears empty and marked as incomplete |
| 2 | 2. Click on the save button | An inline validation error is displayed for the required field |
| 3 | 3. Observe the save action | Settings are not saved and no success message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Saving is blocked and a clear error message is shown for required fields.

---

### TC-239: Prevent saving with invalid input values

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Validate that invalid inputs trigger errors and block saving.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an invalid value into a field with constraints (e.g., max members = -1 or text in numeric field) | The field shows invalid state or accepts input pending validation |
| 2 | 2. Click on the save button | A clear validation error message is displayed for the invalid field |
| 3 | 3. Check that the form remains in edit mode | No settings are saved and user remains on the settings page |

**Final Expected Result:** Invalid values are rejected with understandable error messages and saving is prevented.

---

### TC-240: Boundary validation for numeric limits

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Verify boundary conditions for numeric settings (e.g., member limit).

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page
- Numeric setting has defined min/max constraints

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Set the numeric field to its minimum allowed value | The value is accepted without error |
| 2 | 2. Save the settings | Settings are saved successfully |
| 3 | 3. Set the numeric field to its maximum allowed value | The value is accepted without error |
| 4 | 4. Save the settings | Settings are saved successfully |
| 5 | 5. Set the numeric field below minimum (min-1) | The field is flagged as invalid |
| 6 | 6. Attempt to save | An error is shown and saving is blocked |
| 7 | 7. Set the numeric field above maximum (max+1) | The field is flagged as invalid |
| 8 | 8. Attempt to save | An error is shown and saving is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Only values within defined limits are accepted and saved.

---

### TC-241: Apply restrictive security option and update access rights

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Ensure enabling a restrictive security option limits access and is logged.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page
- A security-relevant setting is available (e.g., 'Only admins can invite')
- A non-admin group member exists for verification

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enable a restrictive security option | The option is toggled on and reflected in the UI |
| 2 | 2. Save the settings | A success confirmation message is displayed |
| 3 | 3. As a non-admin member, attempt the now-restricted action (e.g., invite member) | Access is denied or action is hidden/disabled |
| 4 | 4. Check system audit/protocol logs for the change | A log entry exists showing the security option change with timestamp and admin user |

**Final Expected Result:** Restrictive option is applied immediately, access is limited, and the change is logged.

---

### TC-242: Only group administrators can modify settings

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Verify that non-admin users cannot access or save group settings.

**Preconditions:**
- Non-admin group member is logged in
- User attempts to access Gruppeneinstellungen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Gruppeneinstellungen page URL directly | Access is denied or user is redirected |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to change any setting if page loads | Fields are read-only or changes are blocked |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to save changes | Saving is blocked with an authorization error |

**Final Expected Result:** Only administrators can modify and save group settings.

---

### TC-243: Error message clarity for invalid inputs

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Ensure validation errors are understandable and actionable.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an invalid value in a constrained field | The field is marked invalid after save attempt |
| 2 | 2. Click save | Error message clearly states what is wrong and how to fix it |

**Final Expected Result:** Validation errors are clear, specific, and guide correction.

---

### TC-244: Immediate effect of settings on dependent UI

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-028
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-003

**Description:** Check that UI elements change immediately after saving settings.

**Preconditions:**
- Group admin user is logged in
- User is on Gruppeneinstellungen page
- A setting affects UI visibility (e.g., show/hide group description)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Toggle a setting that controls a UI element | The toggle reflects the new state |
| 2 | 2. Save settings | Success message is shown |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to the group page or refresh it | The UI element is shown/hidden according to the saved setting |

**Final Expected Result:** Settings are immediately reflected in dependent UI.

---

### TC-245: Generate invitation link as group admin

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Verify that a logged-in group admin can generate a unique invitation link

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has admin rights in the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group management page for the target group | Group management page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Generate invitation link' | System creates and displays an invitation link |
| 3 | 3. Copy the generated link | Link is copied to clipboard or displayed for sharing |

**Final Expected Result:** A unique, valid invitation link is generated and ready to be shared

---

### TC-246: Invitation link uniqueness across multiple generations

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Ensure each generated invitation link is unique for the group

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has admin rights in the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Generate an invitation link | First invitation link is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Generate a second invitation link | Second invitation link is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Compare the two links | Links are not identical |

**Final Expected Result:** Each generation produces a distinct invitation link

---

### TC-247: Non-admin cannot generate invitation link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Ensure a user without admin rights cannot generate a group invitation link

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is a group member without admin rights

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group management page | Group management page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to access or click 'Generate invitation link' | Option is hidden or access is denied |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents non-admins from generating invitation links

---

### TC-248: Join group via valid invitation link

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Verify a user can join a group using a valid invitation link

**Preconditions:**
- A valid invitation link exists
- User is logged in and not currently a member of the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the valid invitation link | Join group page or confirmation prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Confirm the join action | System processes the request |
| 3 | 3. Check group membership status | User appears as a member of the group |

**Final Expected Result:** User is added to the group and a confirmation message is displayed

---

### TC-249: Open invitation link without confirmation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Ensure user is not added to the group unless join is confirmed

**Preconditions:**
- A valid invitation link exists
- User is logged in and not currently a member of the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the valid invitation link | Join group page or confirmation prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Dismiss or cancel the confirmation prompt | No join action is performed |
| 3 | 3. Check group membership status | User is not listed as a member of the group |

**Final Expected Result:** User is not added without explicit confirmation

---

### TC-250: Access revoked invitation link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Verify access is denied when an invitation link has been revoked

- User is logged in and not currently a member of the group

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the revoked invitation link | System detects link is revoked |

**Final Expected Result:** System denies access and displays an appropriate error message

---

### TC-251: Access expired invitation link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Verify access is denied when an invitation link has expired

**Preconditions:**
- An invitation link exists and is expired
- User is logged in and not currently a member of the group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the expired invitation link | System detects link is expired |

**Final Expected Result:** System denies access and displays an appropriate error message

---

### TC-252: Invalid or malformed invitation link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Ensure system rejects malformed or tampered invitation links

- User is logged in

### TC-253: Reuse valid invitation link by multiple users

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Verify that multiple different users can join using the same valid invitation link if allowed

- Two users are logged in separately and are not members of the group

|---|--------|------------------|

### TC-254: Attempt to join group when already a member

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-029
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-004

**Description:** Ensure proper handling when a user already belongs to the group

**Preconditions:**
- A valid invitation link exists
- User is logged in and already a member of the group

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the invitation link | System detects user is already a member |
| 2 | 2. Confirm join if prompted | System prevents duplicate membership |

---

### TC-255: TC-01 Erfolgreich Gruppe verlassen als Mitglied

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein Gruppenmitglied die Gruppe verlassen kann und keine Benachrichtigung an andere gesendet wird

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist Mitglied der Gruppe G
- Mindestens ein weiteres Mitglied (Benutzer B) ist in Gruppe G
- Benachrichtigungs-/Aktivitaetslog ist einsehbar

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigiere zur Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G | Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Waehle die Option 'Gruppe verlassen' | Bestaetigungsdialog zum Verlassen der Gruppe wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Bestaetige das Verlassen der Gruppe | Der Vorgang wird verarbeitet und eine Erfolgsmeldung wird angezeigt |
| 4 | 4. Ueberpruefe die Gruppenmitgliedschaft von Benutzer A | Benutzer A ist kein Mitglied der Gruppe G mehr |
| 5 | 5. Pruefe Benachrichtigungen/Aktivitaetslog bei Benutzer B | Keine Benachrichtigung ueber das Verlassen von Benutzer A ist vorhanden |

**Final Expected Result:** Benutzer A wurde aus Gruppe G entfernt und es wurde keine Benachrichtigung an andere Mitglieder gesendet

---

### TC-256: TC-02 Abbruch des Verlassens im Bestaetigungsdialog

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass beim Abbruch keine Aenderung der Mitgliedschaft erfolgt

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist Mitglied der Gruppe G

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigiere zur Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G | Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Waehle die Option 'Gruppe verlassen' | Bestaetigungsdialog wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Klicke auf 'Abbrechen' | Dialog wird geschlossen und keine Erfolgsmeldung wird angezeigt |
| 4 | 4. Ueberpruefe die Gruppenmitgliedschaft von Benutzer A | Benutzer A ist weiterhin Mitglied der Gruppe G |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Verlassen-Vorgang wird abgebrochen und die Mitgliedschaft bleibt bestehen

---

### TC-257: TC-03 Versuch Gruppe zu verlassen, ohne Mitglied zu sein

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass eine verstaendliche Meldung angezeigt wird und keine Aenderung erfolgt

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist kein Mitglied der Gruppe G

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche, die Gruppe G zu verlassen (z.B. ueber Direktlink oder Gruppenliste) | System verarbeitet den Versuch |
| 2 | 2. Beobachte die Rueckmeldung des Systems | Eine verstaendliche Meldung wird angezeigt, dass der Benutzer kein Mitglied ist |
| 3 | 3. Ueberpruefe, dass keine Mitgliedschaftsaenderung vorgenommen wurde | Benutzer A bleibt kein Mitglied der Gruppe G |

**Final Expected Result:** System zeigt eine verstaendliche Meldung und nimmt keine Aenderungen vor

---

### TC-258: TC-04 Technischer Fehler beim Verlassen der Gruppe

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass bei technischem Fehler eine Fehlermeldung erscheint und die Mitgliedschaft bestehen bleibt

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist Mitglied der Gruppe G
- Backend/Service kann simuliert fehlschlagen (z.B. 500-Fehler)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigiere zur Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G | Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Waehle die Option 'Gruppe verlassen' | Bestaetigungsdialog wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Bestaetige das Verlassen, waehrend ein technischer Fehler simuliert wird | System zeigt eine Fehlermeldung an |
| 4 | 4. Ueberpruefe die Gruppenmitgliedschaft von Benutzer A | Benutzer A bleibt Mitglied der Gruppe G |

**Final Expected Result:** Bei Fehler wird eine Fehlermeldung angezeigt und die Mitgliedschaft bleibt erhalten

---

### TC-259: TC-05 Keine Benachrichtigungen beim Verlassen (Audit/Log-Check)

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Stellt sicher, dass das Verlassen keine Benachrichtigungen oder Activity-Events an andere Mitglieder ausloest

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist Mitglied der Gruppe G
- Benutzer B ist Mitglied der Gruppe G
- Zugriff auf Benachrichtigungs- oder Event-Logs fuer Benutzer B ist moeglich

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Benutzer A verlaesst Gruppe G ueber die UI | Benutzer A erhaelt eine Erfolgsmeldung |
| 2 | 2. Pruefe die Benachrichtigungen/Events bei Benutzer B | Kein Event/keine Benachrichtigung ueber das Verlassen von Benutzer A vorhanden |

**Final Expected Result:** Es werden keine Benachrichtigungen oder Events an andere Mitglieder gesendet

---

### TC-260: TC-06 Doppelklick/Mehrfachausfuehrung auf 'Gruppe verlassen'

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Boundary-Test fuer Mehrfachklicks und Idempotenz des Verlassen-Vorgangs

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist Mitglied der Gruppe G

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigiere zur Gruppendetailseite von Gruppe G | Gruppendetailseite wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Oeffne den Bestaetigungsdialog und klicke schnell mehrfach auf 'Bestaetigen' | Vorgang wird nur einmal verarbeitet oder nach dem ersten Klick deaktiviert |
| 3 | 3. Ueberpruefe die Gruppenmitgliedschaft von Benutzer A | Benutzer A ist kein Mitglied der Gruppe G mehr |
| 4 | 4. Pruefe Systemmeldungen | Keine widerspruechlichen Fehlermeldungen; maximal eine Erfolgsmeldung |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Vorgang ist idempotent und fuehrt nicht zu Fehlzustaenden

---

### TC-261: TC-07 Verlassen ueber Direkt-URL/Deep Link

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-030
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-005

**Description:** Negativtest fuer ungueltigen oder direkten Zugriff auf Verlassen-Endpunkt ohne Mitgliedschaft

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Benutzer A ist kein Mitglied der Gruppe G
- Direkter Verlassen-Endpunkt/URL ist bekannt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Rufe den Direkt-Link/Endpunkt zum Verlassen von Gruppe G auf | System verarbeitet den Request |
| 2 | 2. Beobachte die Rueckmeldung des Systems | Verstaendliche Meldung oder Fehlerhinweis, dass keine Mitgliedschaft besteht |
| 3 | 3. Ueberpruefe, dass keine Aenderung der Gruppenmitgliedschaft vorgenommen wurde | Benutzer A bleibt kein Mitglied der Gruppe G |

**Final Expected Result:** Kein Verlassen moeglich ohne Mitgliedschaft; keine Aenderung am Systemzustand

---

### TC-262: Create community with multiple groups

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify that a community can be created with multiple groups and groups are uniquely assigned

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Community creation page | Community creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid community name and details | Community details are accepted with no validation errors |
| 3 | 3. Add Group A and Group B | Both groups appear in the group list for the community |
| 4 | 4. Click Save/Create | Community is saved successfully |
| 5 | 5. Open the created community details | Group A and Group B are shown as uniquely associated to the community |

**Final Expected Result:** Community is created and all added groups are uniquely associated with it

---

### TC-263: Remove a single group from an existing community

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify removing one group does not affect other groups

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to manage communities
- A community exists with Group A, Group B, Group C

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the existing community details | Community details display Group A, Group B, Group C |
| 2 | 2. Remove Group B from the community | Group B is removed from the list |
| 3 | 3. Save changes | Changes are saved successfully |
| 4 | 4. Refresh or reopen community details | Group A and Group C remain, Group B is absent |

**Final Expected Result:** Only the selected group is removed while other groups remain unchanged

---

### TC-264: Attempt to create community without adding any groups

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify error is shown and community is not created when no groups are added

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Community creation page | Community creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid community name and details | Community details are accepted with no validation errors |
| 3 | 3. Do not add any group | Group list remains empty |
| 4 | 4. Click Save/Create | Error message is displayed indicating at least one group is required |

**Final Expected Result:** Community is not created and an error is shown due to missing groups

---

### TC-265: Boundary test: Create community with exactly one group

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify community can be created with minimum valid number of groups (1)

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Community creation page | Community creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid community name and details | Community details are accepted with no validation errors |
| 3 | 3. Add exactly one group | Single group appears in the group list |
| 4 | 4. Click Save/Create | Community is saved successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Community is created successfully with one group

---

### TC-266: Prevent duplicate group association within a community

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify the same group cannot be added twice to the same community

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Community creation page | Community creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Add Group A | Group A appears in the group list |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to add Group A again | System prevents duplicate addition or shows validation message |

**Final Expected Result:** Duplicate group is not added to the community

### TC-267: Authorization: user without permission cannot create community

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify access control for community creation

- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User does not have permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to access Community creation page | Access is denied or user is redirected with an authorization error |

**Final Expected Result:** User without permission cannot create a community

### TC-268: Remove group and verify other group data unchanged

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify other groups' attributes remain unchanged after removal of one group

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to manage communities
- A community exists with Group A (name and description), Group B (name and description)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open community details | Group A and Group B details are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Remove Group B | Group B is removed from list |
| 3 | 3. Save changes | Changes are saved successfully |
| 4 | 4. Verify Group A details | Group A details remain unchanged |

**Final Expected Result:** Only the selected group is removed; remaining group data is unchanged

### TC-269: Integration: community-group association persisted in backend

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** Verify persistence of community-group associations after creation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a community with Group A and Group B | Community is created successfully |
| 2 | 2. Retrieve community via API or backend datastore | Community record contains associations to Group A and Group B |

**Final Expected Result:** Community-group relationships are persisted correctly in backend

---

### TC-270: E2E: create community, remove group, and verify UI consistency

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-031
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-006

**Description:** End-to-end verification of create and remove flows in UI

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Endnutzer
- User has permission to create and manage communities

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a community with Group A and Group B | Community is created and groups are listed |
| 2 | 2. Remove Group B and save | Group B disappears from list and changes save |
| 3 | 3. Navigate away and return to community details | Only Group A is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** UI reflects correct community-group state across navigation

### TC-271: Create one-way broadcast channel and send message as verified business user

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Verify that a verified business user with channel management permission can create a one-way broadcast channel and send a message to all subscribers

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has verified Business profile
- User has channel management permission
- No existing broadcast channel with the same name
- At least two subscribers are available to subscribe

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to channel management | Channel management page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Create a new One-Way Broadcast channel with valid name and settings | Channel is created successfully and appears in channel list |
| 3 | 3. Add/confirm subscribers to the channel | Subscribers are added and visible in channel subscriber list |
| 4 | 4. Publish a message in the channel | Message is accepted and queued for delivery |
| 5 | 5. Open channel history | Published message appears in the channel history |
| 6 | 6. Verify message delivery for each subscriber | Each subscriber receives the message successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Broadcast message is delivered to all subscribers and shown in channel history

---

### TC-272: Subscriber blocked from posting in one-way broadcast channel

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Ensure subscribers cannot post messages in a one-way broadcast channel and receive appropriate error feedback

**Preconditions:**
- One-way broadcast channel exists
- User is logged in as a subscriber (non-operator) of the channel

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast channel as a subscriber | Channel view loads with message history |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to post a message in the input area | Send action is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system feedback | Error message indicates only the channel operator can send messages |

**Final Expected Result:** Subscriber cannot post and receives a clear restriction message

---

### TC-273: Sending broadcast message without sufficient permission is rejected

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Validate that a user without sufficient permission cannot publish a broadcast message and the error is logged

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User does not have channel management permission
- One-way broadcast channel exists
- Logging/monitoring is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to publish a message in the broadcast channel via UI/API | Request is rejected by authorization layer |
| 2 | 2. Check user-facing response | User sees permission error message |
| 3 | 3. Verify security log entry | Permission error is recorded with user ID and timestamp |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is not sent and a permission error is logged

---

### TC-274: Non-verified business user cannot create one-way broadcast channel

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Confirm that users without verified Business profile cannot create a broadcast channel

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User does not have verified Business profile
- User attempts to access channel management

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to channel management | Access is denied or restricted options are shown |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to create a one-way broadcast channel | Creation action is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Review system feedback | User is informed about verification requirement |

**Final Expected Result:** Channel is not created and the user is informed about missing verification

---

### TC-275: Broadcast message delivery to large subscriber list

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Verify successful delivery to a boundary condition of maximum allowed subscribers

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with verified Business profile
- User has channel management permission
- One-way broadcast channel exists
- Channel has maximum allowed subscribers configured (boundary value)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Publish a message to the channel | Message is accepted for delivery |
| 2 | 2. Monitor delivery status for all subscribers | Delivery completes within acceptable time limits |
| 3 | 3. Check channel history | Message appears once in channel history |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is delivered to all subscribers at boundary limit without failure

---

### TC-276: Empty message cannot be published

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Ensure that an empty broadcast message is rejected with validation error

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with verified Business profile
- User has channel management permission
- One-way broadcast channel exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the broadcast channel as operator | Message input is available |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to send an empty message | Validation error is shown |
| 3 | 3. Check channel history | No new message is added |

**Final Expected Result:** Empty message is not sent and validation error is displayed

---

### TC-277: Message is displayed in channel history for operator and subscribers

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Validate that a sent broadcast message appears in history for both operator and subscriber views

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in with verified Business profile
- User has channel management permission
- One-way broadcast channel exists with at least one subscriber

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Operator sends a valid message | Message is accepted for delivery |
| 2 | 2. Operator opens channel history | Message appears in history with correct timestamp |
| 3 | 3. Subscriber opens channel history | Same message appears in history with correct timestamp |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is visible in history for both operator and subscriber

---

### TC-278: Unauthorized API call to send broadcast message is denied

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-032
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-007

**Description:** Ensure backend rejects unauthenticated or invalid token attempts to send broadcast messages

**Preconditions:**
- One-way broadcast channel exists
- No valid authentication token is provided

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send API request to publish a broadcast message without token | API responds with 401/403 unauthorized |
| 2 | 2. Verify message was not delivered | No subscribers receive the message |
| 3 | 3. Check audit logs | Unauthorized attempt is logged |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthorized API call is denied and logged, no message is delivered

---

### TC-279: Create and send poll in group chat with two options

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-033
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-008

**Description:** Verify that a poll with at least two options can be created and displayed in a group chat and all members can vote

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A is a member of an existing group chat with User B and User C
- Group chat is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A clicks on the "Create Poll" action in the group chat | Poll creation UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. User A enters a poll question and adds two answer options | Question and two options are visible in the poll form |
| 3 | 3. User A clicks "Send" | Poll is posted in the group chat |
| 4 | 4. User B selects one option and submits the vote | User B's vote is recorded and visible according to poll design |
| 5 | 5. User C selects one option and submits the vote | User C's vote is recorded and visible according to poll design |

**Final Expected Result:** The poll is visible in the group chat and all group members can vote successfully

---

### TC-280: Create and send poll in one-to-one chat

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-033
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-008

**Description:** Verify that a poll can be created and displayed in a one-to-one chat and the recipient can vote

- User A is logged in
- User A has an existing one-to-one chat with User B
- One-to-one chat is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A clicks on the "Create Poll" action in the one-to-one chat | Poll creation UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. User A enters a poll question and adds two answer options | Question and two options are visible in the poll form |
| 3 | 3. User A clicks "Send" | Poll is posted in the one-to-one chat |
| 4 | 4. User B selects one option and submits the vote | User B's vote is recorded and visible according to poll design |

**Final Expected Result:** The poll is visible in the one-to-one chat and the recipient can vote successfully

### TC-281: Prevent sending poll without any answer options

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-033
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-008

**Description:** Verify that a poll cannot be sent without options and an error message is shown

- User is logged in
- Any chat (group or one-to-one) is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User opens poll creation UI | Poll creation UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. User enters a poll question but leaves options empty | No options are present in the poll form |
| 3 | 3. User clicks "Send" | An error message is shown and the poll is not sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Poll is not sent and a validation error is displayed

### TC-282: Prevent sending poll with only one option

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-033
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-008

**Description:** Verify that a poll requires at least two options

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Any chat (group or one-to-one) is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User opens poll creation UI | Poll creation UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. User enters a poll question and adds only one answer option | Poll form shows a single option |
| 3 | 3. User clicks "Send" | Validation error indicates at least two options are required and the poll is not sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Poll is not sent and validation requires a minimum of two options

**Status:** manual

**Status:** manual

**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual

### TC-287: Create event with all required fields

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- User is a member of the target group
- Group has at least one other member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group page | Group page is displayed with event creation option |
| 2 | 2. Open the 'Create Event' form | Event creation form is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter a valid title, future date/time, location, and description | All fields accept input without validation errors |
| 4 | 4. Click 'Save' to create the event | Event is saved successfully and creation confirmation is shown |
| 5 | 5. Verify event appears in the group event list | New event is visible with correct details |
| 6 | 6. Verify notifications are sent to all group members | All group members receive a notification about the new event |

**Final Expected Result:** Event is created and displayed in the group, and notifications are sent to all members

### TC-291: Attempt to save event without description

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

**Description:** Validate required field error when description is missing

- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- User is a member of the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the 'Create Event' form | Event creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid title, future date/time, and location; leave description empty | Form accepts input for non-empty fields |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Save' | Validation message is displayed for the description field |

**Final Expected Result:** Event is not saved and a clear validation message indicates description is required

---

### TC-292: Attempt to save event with past date

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

**Description:** Ensure event creation is blocked for dates in the past

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- User is a member of the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the 'Create Event' form | Event creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid title, location, description and set date/time in the past | Past date/time is accepted in the input field |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Save' | Error message indicates past dates are not allowed |

**Final Expected Result:** Event is not saved and a clear error message is shown for past date/time

---

### TC-293: Boundary: event date/time equals current time

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

**Description:** Verify handling when event date/time is exactly at current time

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- User is a member of the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the 'Create Event' form | Event creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid title, location, description and set date/time to current system time | Current time is accepted in the input field |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Save' | System validates whether current time is allowed |

**Final Expected Result:** Event is saved only if current time is considered valid; otherwise a clear error is shown

---

### TC-294: Create event with minimal valid inputs

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

**Description:** Verify that only required fields are sufficient to create an event

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- User is a member of the target group

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the 'Create Event' form | Event creation form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Fill only the required fields with valid values | Form accepts all required inputs |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Save' | Event is created successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Event is created and displayed with required details

---

### TC-295: Notification delivery to all group members

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-034
**Requirement:** WA-GRP-009

**Description:** Validate that all members receive notification after event creation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as group member with event creation permission
- Group has multiple members with notifications enabled
- Notification service is operational

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Create a valid event in the group | Event is saved successfully |
| 2 | 2. Check notification inbox for each group member | Each member has a new notification about the event |

**Final Expected Result:** All group members receive a notification for the new event

---

### TC-296: Encrypted voice call setup with reachable recipient

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify a voice call can be started and remains end-to-end encrypted when recipient is reachable

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to recipient profile or chat | Recipient contact options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap the voice call button | Call initiation screen appears and dialing starts |
| 3 | 3. Recipient answers the call | Call connects and audio stream starts |
| 4 | 4. Inspect call security indicator | UI shows encryption is active for the call |
| 5 | 5. Exchange audio for 30 seconds | Audio is clear and uninterrupted during the test window |

**Final Expected Result:** The call is successfully established and the voice transmission remains encrypted throughout

---

### TC-297: Recipient rejects the voice call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify caller is notified when recipient declines the call

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a voice call to the recipient | Dialing UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Recipient declines the incoming call | Caller receives a decline notification |

**Final Expected Result:** Caller sees a clear status indicating the call was declined

---

### TC-298: Recipient is not reachable

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify caller is notified when the recipient is offline/unreachable

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is offline or unreachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a voice call to the recipient | Dialing UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Wait for the call attempt to time out | Caller receives an unreachable status notification |

**Final Expected Result:** Caller sees a clear status indicating the recipient is not reachable

---

### TC-299: Unstable network with packet loss during encrypted call

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify system attempts to stabilize and informs user on call drop

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable
- Network can be throttled to simulate packet loss

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish an encrypted voice call | Call connects with encryption active |
| 2 | 2. Introduce 10-20% packet loss on the caller network | System attempts to recover and maintains the call if possible |
| 3 | 3. Increase packet loss to 40-60% for 20 seconds | Audio quality degrades and system continues recovery attempts |
| 4 | 4. Maintain severe packet loss until the call drops | Call disconnects and user is informed of the call drop |

**Final Expected Result:** System tries to stabilize the call and notifies user upon call termination due to network instability

---

### TC-300: Encryption indicator not shown when call is unencrypted (negative)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Ensure call does not proceed if encryption cannot be established

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable
- Force encryption negotiation failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to start a voice call with forced encryption failure | Call setup fails or prompts an error |
| 2 | 2. Observe call status | User receives a clear message that secure call cannot be established |

**Final Expected Result:** Call does not proceed without encryption and user is notified

---

### TC-301: Call setup time within acceptable threshold

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify call setup latency meets performance expectations under stable network

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start a voice call and measure time to connection | Call connects within defined SLA (e.g., <= 5 seconds) |

**Final Expected Result:** Call setup time meets performance threshold

---

### TC-302: User cancels call during dialing

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify cancellation during dialing stops call and provides status

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a voice call | Dialing UI appears |
| 2 | 2. Tap cancel/end call before recipient answers | Dialing stops and call is terminated |
| 3 | 3. Observe call status | Caller sees a status indicating the call was cancelled |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is cancelled cleanly and user sees accurate status

---

### TC-303: Boundary: recipient becomes unreachable during ringing

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify correct notification when recipient disconnects while call is ringing

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable initially

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a voice call to the recipient | Dialing UI appears and ringing starts |
| 2 | 2. Simulate recipient going offline during ringing | Call attempt fails |
| 3 | 3. Observe call status | Caller receives a clear unreachable status notification |

**Final Expected Result:** Caller is notified that the recipient became unreachable

---

### TC-304: Audio continuity under mild packet loss

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Ensure audio remains usable with mild packet loss and recovery mechanisms

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Recipient is logged in and reachable
- Network can be throttled to simulate packet loss

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish an encrypted voice call | Call connects with encryption active |
| 2 | 2. Introduce 5% packet loss for 60 seconds | Audio remains intelligible and call stays connected |
| 3 | 3. Remove packet loss and continue call | Audio quality returns to normal |

**Final Expected Result:** System stabilizes the connection and maintains call quality under mild loss

---

### TC-305: Establish encrypted video call between two online users

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify that a video call can be initiated and accepted with end-to-end encryption and both audio/video streams active.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online on supported platforms
- Both users have granted camera and microphone permissions

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A opens User B profile and taps 'Video Call' | Outbound call screen is shown and call request is sent to User B |
| 2 | 2. User B receives the incoming video call notification | Incoming call UI is displayed with accept/decline options |
| 3 | 3. User B taps 'Accept' | Call connects and video call UI is displayed for both users |
| 4 | 4. Verify encryption status indicator in call UI | End-to-end encryption indicator is shown as active |
| 5 | 5. Confirm both users can see and hear each other | Two-way audio and video are clear and in sync |

**Final Expected Result:** Video call is established with end-to-end encryption and both users can see and hear each other.

---

### TC-306: Video call attempt when recipient is offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Ensure system shows not available notification and does not start a call when recipient is offline.

**Preconditions:**
- User A has a valid account and is online
- User B has a valid account and is offline

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | System checks User B availability status |
| 2 | 2. Observe the call initiation response | Not available notification is displayed to User A |
| 3 | 3. Check call state | Call is not started and no ringing occurs |

**Final Expected Result:** User A receives a not available notification and the call does not start.

---

### TC-307: Video call attempt when recipient is busy or not available

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Validate that the system prevents call start and shows not available when recipient is busy.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- User A is online
- User B is online but in another active call

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | System checks User B availability |
| 2 | 2. Observe notification to User A | Not available or busy notification is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Verify call state | Call is not started |

**Final Expected Result:** User A is informed that User B is not available and the call is not started.

---

### TC-308: Reconnection attempt on unstable network during active call

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Confirm the system attempts to reconnect and informs the user on failure.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- An active video call is established between User A and User B

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simulate unstable network for User A (packet loss/high latency) | Call quality degrades and reconnection attempt starts |
| 2 | 2. Observe UI during reconnection attempt | User A sees a reconnecting indicator |
| 3 | 3. Simulate complete network loss for User A for a sustained period | Reconnection attempt continues for a defined timeout |
| 4 | 4. Observe outcome after timeout | User A receives a clear failure message and call ends |

**Final Expected Result:** System attempts reconnection and informs the user clearly upon failure.

---

### TC-309: Successful reconnection after brief network interruption

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Validate that a brief network drop triggers reconnection and resumes the call.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- An active video call is established between User A and User B

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Disconnect User A network for 5 seconds | Reconnecting indicator is shown |
| 2 | 2. Restore User A network | System reconnects and restores audio/video streams |
| 3 | 3. Verify call continuity | Call remains active and audio/video resume within acceptable time |

**Final Expected Result:** Call recovers after brief interruption with audio/video restored.

---

### TC-310: Cross-platform call between different device types

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Ensure encrypted video call works across platforms (e.g., iOS to Android or Web).

**Preconditions:**
- User A on Platform X and User B on Platform Y have valid accounts
- Both users are online and permissions granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | User B receives incoming call UI on Platform Y |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connects across platforms |
| 3 | 3. Verify encryption indicator on both platforms | End-to-end encryption indicator is active on both |
| 4 | 4. Validate audio/video quality | Both users can see and hear each other |

**Final Expected Result:** Encrypted video call is established and functional across platforms.

---

### TC-311: Call initiation with revoked camera/microphone permissions

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Ensure user is prompted appropriately and call is not started without permissions.

**Preconditions:**
- User A has a valid account and is online
- User B has a valid account and is online
- User A has denied camera and microphone permissions

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | Permission prompt or error is shown to User A |
| 2 | 2. User A does not grant permissions | Call is not started and User B is not notified |

**Final Expected Result:** System blocks call start and informs User A to grant permissions.

---

### TC-312: Recipient rejects incoming call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify that rejecting a call does not establish a session and caller is notified.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | User B receives incoming call UI |
| 2 | 2. User B taps 'Decline' | User A is notified that the call was declined |
| 3 | 3. Verify call state on both devices | No call session is established |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined and no session is created.

---

### TC-313: Call setup timeout when recipient does not respond

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Validate that call times out and caller is informed when recipient does not answer.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online
- User B does not interact with the incoming call

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | Outgoing call UI shows ringing status |
| 2 | 2. Wait for the configured timeout duration | Call attempt ends automatically |
| 3 | 3. Observe notification to User A | No answer or timeout message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Call attempt times out and caller is notified.

---

### TC-314: Encryption status not established (negative)

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Ensure call fails or is blocked if end-to-end encryption cannot be established.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online
- Simulate encryption handshake failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a video call to User B | System attempts encryption handshake |
| 2 | 2. Inject encryption negotiation failure | Call is blocked or immediately terminated |
| 3 | 3. Observe error message | User sees clear message indicating secure connection could not be established |

**Final Expected Result:** Call does not proceed without end-to-end encryption and user is informed.

---

### TC-315: High quality video call under normal network conditions

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Validate video quality remains high with stable bandwidth and latency.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online
- Stable network with sufficient bandwidth and low latency

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish a video call between User A and User B | Call connects successfully with encryption enabled |
| 2 | 2. Monitor video resolution and frame rate for 5 minutes | Video maintains target resolution and stable frame rate |
| 3 | 3. Monitor audio latency and synchronization | Audio remains synchronized with video without noticeable delay |

**Final Expected Result:** Call maintains high video quality and synchronized audio under stable conditions.

---

### TC-316: Boundary test: minimum bandwidth for call continuity

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Assess behavior at the lower threshold of supported bandwidth.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B have valid accounts
- Both users are online
- Network bandwidth is throttled to the minimum supported level

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish a video call between User A and User B | Call connects with possible reduced quality |
| 2 | 2. Observe adaptive bitrate behavior | System lowers video quality but maintains call stability |
| 3 | 3. Verify user notification (if applicable) | User is informed of reduced quality if supported |

**Final Expected Result:** Call remains connected with adaptive quality at minimum bandwidth.

---

### TC-317: Start group voice call with selected contacts

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can start a group voice call and all invitees are notified

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- At least 2 contacts are available and online
- Microphone permission granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open contacts list and select 2 or more contacts | Selected contacts are highlighted and added to the invite list |
| 2 | Choose 'Voice Call' as the media type | Voice call option is selected and video is not selected |
| 3 | Tap 'Start Call' | Call setup begins and invites are sent to all selected contacts |
| 4 | Observe invitees' devices | Each invitee receives a call notification for a group voice call |

**Final Expected Result:** Group voice call is initiated with voice media and all invitees are notified

---

### TC-318: Start group video call with selected contacts

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can start a group video call and all invitees are notified

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- At least 2 contacts are available and online
- Camera and microphone permissions granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open contacts list and select 3 contacts | Selected contacts are added to the invite list |
| 2 | Choose 'Video Call' as the media type | Video call option is selected |
| 3 | Tap 'Start Call' | Call setup begins and invites are sent to all selected contacts |
| 4 | Observe invitees' devices | Each invitee receives a call notification for a group video call |

**Final Expected Result:** Group video call is initiated with video media and all invitees are notified

---

### TC-319: Add participant to active group call without interrupting others

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify that an invited participant can join an active group call seamlessly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An active group call with at least 2 participants exists
- A new contact is available to invite

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | From the active call screen, choose 'Add Participant' | Invite UI opens with contact list |
| 2 | Select a new contact and send invite | Invite is sent to the selected contact |
| 3 | On the invitee device, accept the invitation | Invitee begins joining the call |
| 4 | Monitor call audio/video for existing participants | No interruption or drop occurs for existing participants |

**Final Expected Result:** New participant joins the active call without disrupting existing participants

---

### TC-320: Auto-reconnect on unstable network during group call join

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify automatic reconnection attempts and error message on failure

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on a device with unstable network
- An active group call invite is received

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Accept the group call invitation | System attempts to connect to the call |
| 2 | Simulate network instability causing connection failure | System automatically starts reconnection attempts |
| 3 | Allow reconnection attempts to exhaust without restoring network | A clear, user-friendly error message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** System retries connection automatically and shows an understandable error message upon failure

---

### TC-321: Auto-reconnect on dropped connection while in group call

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify reconnection behavior when connection drops mid-call

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is actively in a group call
- Network can be toggled to unstable/off

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
