| 1 | Disable or severely degrade network connectivity | Call connection drops for the participant |
| 2 | Observe the system behavior | System attempts automatic reconnection |
| 3 | Restore network connectivity | Participant reconnects to the group call |

**Final Expected Result:** Participant is automatically reconnected after network recovery

---

### TC-322: Start group call with minimum participants (boundary)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify behavior when only one contact is selected (minimum group size boundary)

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- At least 1 contact is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Select only one contact from the list | One contact is added to the invite list |
| 2 | Tap 'Start Call' | System either starts a 1:1 call or shows a message indicating a minimum group size is required |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles the minimum boundary appropriately (either allows 1:1 or provides a clear constraint message)

---

### TC-323: Start group call with maximum allowed participants (boundary)

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify system behavior at maximum participant limit

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Maximum participant limit is known and contacts are available to meet the limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Select contacts up to the maximum allowed limit | All contacts up to the limit are added to the invite list |
| 2 | Attempt to add one more contact beyond the limit | System prevents adding and shows a clear limit message |
| 3 | Start the call | Call is initiated and invites are sent to the selected contacts |

**Final Expected Result:** System enforces participant limit and successfully starts call within the limit

---

### TC-324: Invitee declines group call invitation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify the caller is informed when an invitee declines

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Caller starts a group call with at least 2 invitees

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Invitee taps 'Decline' on the call notification | Invitee does not join the call |
| 2 | Observe caller's call screen | Caller sees decline status for the invitee |

**Final Expected Result:** Declined invitations are reflected to the caller without affecting the ongoing call

---

### TC-325: Non-logged-in user attempts to start group call (negative)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify that unauthenticated users cannot start a group call

**Preconditions:**
- User is not logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to group call feature entry point | User is prompted to log in |
| 2 | Attempt to start a group call without logging in | Call initiation is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthenticated users are prevented from starting group calls

---

### TC-326: Start call without selecting contacts (negative)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify validation when no contacts are selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open group call screen without selecting any contacts | Invite list is empty |
| 2 | Tap 'Start Call' | System blocks action and shows validation message |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents call start and displays a clear message to select contacts

---

### TC-327: Media type selection persists for call setup

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify chosen media type (voice/video) is used when call starts

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- At least 2 contacts are selected

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Select 'Voice Call' and start call | Call connects with audio only |
| 2 | End call, then select 'Video Call' and start a new call | Call connects with audio and video |

**Final Expected Result:** Call media type matches user selection each time

---

### TC-328: Cross-platform invite notification delivery

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify invite notifications are received on different platforms

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in on Platform A
- Invitees are logged in on Platform B and C
- Contacts are selected

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Caller starts a group call | Invites are sent |
| 2 | Check notifications on Platform B and C devices | Both devices receive group call notifications |

**Final Expected Result:** Group call notifications are delivered across platforms

---

### TC-329: Start Bildschirmfreigabe während aktivem Anruf

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass eine Bildschirmfreigabe während eines aktiven Anrufs gestartet werden kann und Teilnehmer die Ansicht in Echtzeit sehen

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht
- Beide Teilnehmer haben stabile Netzwerkverbindung

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A öffnet das In-Call-Menü | Das In-Call-Menü mit der Option „Bildschirmfreigabe starten“ wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer A klickt auf „Bildschirmfreigabe starten“ | Das Betriebssystem-Auswahlfenster für Bildschirm/Fenster-Freigabe erscheint |
| 3 | 3. Nutzer A wählt „Gesamter Bildschirm“ und bestätigt | Die Freigabe startet und ein Indikator „Bildschirmfreigabe aktiv“ wird bei Nutzer A angezeigt |
| 4 | 4. Nutzer B beobachtet den Anrufbildschirm | Nutzer B sieht die geteilte Ansicht in Echtzeit |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Bildschirmfreigabe startet erfolgreich und alle Teilnehmer sehen die geteilte Ansicht in Echtzeit

---

### TC-330: Bildschirmfreigabe beenden

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass das Beenden der Freigabe die Übertragung sofort stoppt und eine Statusansicht angezeigt wird

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht
- Eine Bildschirmfreigabe ist aktiv

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A klickt auf „Freigabe beenden“ | Die Schaltfläche bestätigt die Aktion (z. B. Statuswechsel oder Modal) |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer B beobachtet den Anrufbildschirm | Die geteilte Ansicht wird sofort gestoppt und die Ansicht „Freigabe beendet“ erscheint |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Freigabe wird sofort beendet und alle Teilnehmer sehen „Freigabe beendet“

---

### TC-331: Fehlermeldung bei fehlender OS-Berechtigung

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass bei fehlender Berechtigung eine verständliche Fehlermeldung erscheint und keine Freigabe startet

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht
- OS-Berechtigung für Bildschirmfreigabe ist entzogen oder nicht erteilt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A klickt auf „Bildschirmfreigabe starten“ | Das System versucht die Freigabe zu initiieren |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer A verweigert die OS-Berechtigungsanfrage | Eine verständliche Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Keine Bildschirmfreigabe wird gestartet und eine verständliche Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt

---

### TC-332: Abbruch der OS-Auswahl ohne Freigabe

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Negativtest: Verifiziert, dass beim Abbruch der OS-Auswahl keine Freigabe startet und der Status stabil bleibt

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A klickt auf „Bildschirmfreigabe starten“ | Das OS-Auswahlfenster wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer A schließt das OS-Auswahlfenster ohne Auswahl | Keine Bildschirmfreigabe startet und kein Freigabe-Indikator erscheint |
| 3 | 3. Nutzer B beobachtet den Anrufbildschirm | Kein Wechsel zur geteilten Ansicht |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Freigabe startet nicht und der Anruf bleibt unverändert

---

### TC-333: Freigabe eines einzelnen Fensters

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein einzelnes Fenster korrekt freigegeben wird

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A öffnet das OS-Auswahlfenster für die Freigabe | Auswahloptionen für Bildschirm oder Fenster werden angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer A wählt ein bestimmtes Anwendungsfenster und bestätigt | Die Freigabe startet und der Indikator „Fensterfreigabe aktiv“ erscheint |
| 3 | 3. Nutzer B beobachtet die geteilte Ansicht | Nur das ausgewählte Fenster ist sichtbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Das ausgewählte Fenster wird korrekt geteilt und in Echtzeit angezeigt

---

### TC-334: Wechsel von Fenster- zu Bildschirmfreigabe in derselben Sitzung

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Boundary/Flow-Test: Verifiziert, dass Nutzer die Freigabe beenden und eine neue Freigabe starten können

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht
- Eine Fensterfreigabe ist aktiv

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A beendet die aktuelle Fensterfreigabe | Die Freigabe endet und „Freigabe beendet“ wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer A startet erneut die Bildschirmfreigabe | Das OS-Auswahlfenster wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Nutzer A wählt „Gesamter Bildschirm“ und bestätigt | Die Bildschirmfreigabe startet und wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Nutzer kann die Freigabe beenden und eine neue Freigabe korrekt starten

---

### TC-335: Mehrere Teilnehmer sehen Freigabe in Echtzeit

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verifiziert Echtzeit-Übertragung bei mehr als zwei Teilnehmern

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierte Nutzer A, B und C sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Gruppenanruf zwischen A, B und C besteht

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A startet die Bildschirmfreigabe und wählt „Gesamter Bildschirm“ | Die Freigabe startet und ein Indikator „Bildschirmfreigabe aktiv“ erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer B beobachtet die geteilte Ansicht | Die geteilte Ansicht ist in Echtzeit sichtbar |
| 3 | 3. Nutzer C beobachtet die geteilte Ansicht | Die geteilte Ansicht ist in Echtzeit sichtbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Alle Teilnehmer sehen die Freigabe in Echtzeit

---

### TC-336: Start Bildschirmfreigabe ohne aktiven Anruf

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Negativtest: Verifiziert, dass Freigabe nicht ohne aktiven Anruf gestartet werden kann

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A ist eingeloggt
- Kein aktiver Anruf

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A versucht die Bildschirmfreigabe aus dem Hauptmenü zu starten | Die Option ist deaktiviert oder eine Hinweismeldung erscheint |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Bildschirmfreigabe wird nicht gestartet und der Nutzer erhält eine passende Hinweismeldung

---

### TC-337: Stabilität der Echtzeit-Übertragung bei hoher CPU-Last

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Performance/Boundary: Verifiziert, dass die Übertragung unter hoher Last weiterhin funktioniert

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf zwischen Nutzer A und Nutzer B besteht
- Nutzer A erzeugt hohe CPU-Last (z. B. Rendering-Aufgabe)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Nutzer A startet die Bildschirmfreigabe | Die Freigabe startet und der Indikator „Bildschirmfreigabe aktiv“ erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Nutzer B beobachtet die geteilte Ansicht über 2 Minuten | Die Ansicht bleibt sichtbar mit akzeptabler Latenz und ohne Abbruch |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Bildschirmfreigabe bleibt unter hoher CPU-Last stabil und sichtbar

---

### TC-338: Wiederherstellung nach kurzfristigem Netzwerkverlust während Freigabe

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Boundary/Integration: Verifiziert Verhalten bei kurzem Netzwerkverlust

**Preconditions:**
- Registrierter Nutzer A und B sind eingeloggt
- Ein aktiver Anruf besteht
- Bildschirmfreigabe ist aktiv

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simuliere einen kurzzeitigen Netzwerkverlust bei Nutzer A (z. B. 5 Sekunden) | Die Freigabe stoppt vorübergehend oder zeigt einen Verbindungsstatus |
| 2 | 2. Stelle die Netzwerkverbindung wieder her | Die Freigabe setzt sich fort oder der Nutzer wird zur erneuten Freigabe aufgefordert |

**Final Expected Result:** Das System behandelt kurzfristige Unterbrechungen stabil und informiert die Teilnehmer angemessen

---

### TC-339: Generate call link for valid scheduled call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify a unique call link is created and displayed for a scheduled call with valid future date/time.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- A scheduled call exists with a valid future date and time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the scheduled call details page | Scheduled call details are displayed with date/time in the future |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Anruflink erzeugen' | System initiates link generation |
| 3 | 3. Observe the generated link area | A unique call link is displayed and marked for secure sharing |

**Final Expected Result:** System generates a unique call link for the scheduled call and displays it for secure sharing.

---

### TC-340: Prevent call link generation for past scheduled call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure the system blocks link creation when the scheduled call start time is in the past.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- A scheduled call exists with a start time in the past

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the scheduled call details page | Scheduled call details are displayed with date/time in the past |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Anruflink erzeugen' | System blocks link creation |
| 3 | 3. Review the error message | A clear, understandable error message is shown indicating the call is in the past |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents call link creation and shows a clear error message.

---

### TC-341: Customer opens valid call link on supported platform

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify a shared valid link routes the customer to the scheduled call and informs about the planned time.

**Preconditions:**
- A valid call link has been generated for a future scheduled call
- Customer has access to a supported platform (e.g., modern browser)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Customer opens the shared call link on a supported platform | Link resolves and loads the call join page |
| 2 | 2. Customer views the call details | Scheduled time and call information are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Customer proceeds to join the call (if within join window) | Customer is routed to the intended call session |

**Final Expected Result:** Customer is directed to the scheduled call and informed about the planned time.

---

### TC-342: Boundary: Generate link for call starting in 1 minute

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Validate link generation for a call with a start time very close in the future.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- A scheduled call exists with a start time 1 minute in the future

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the scheduled call details page | Call details show start time 1 minute ahead |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Anruflink erzeugen' | System generates the link without error |
| 3 | 3. Verify the link is displayed | Unique call link is visible for sharing |

**Final Expected Result:** System successfully generates and displays a unique call link for a near-future call.

---

### TC-343: Boundary: Prevent link generation when start time equals current time

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure link creation is blocked if scheduled start time is at or before the current time.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- A scheduled call exists with start time equal to current time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the scheduled call details page | Call details show start time equal to current time |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Anruflink erzeugen' | System blocks link creation |
| 3 | 3. Review the error message | Error message indicates that link cannot be created for past/started calls |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents link creation and shows a clear error message.

---

### TC-344: Uniqueness of generated call link

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify that the generated link is unique for each scheduled call.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter
- Two distinct scheduled calls exist with future start times

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Generate a call link for Call A | Link A is generated and displayed |
| 2 | 2. Generate a call link for Call B | Link B is generated and displayed |
| 3 | 3. Compare Link A and Link B | Links are different and map to their respective calls |

**Final Expected Result:** Each scheduled call receives a unique call link.

---

### TC-345: Link opens on unsupported platform

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Validate behavior when a customer opens the link on an unsupported platform.

**Preconditions:**
- A valid call link has been generated
- Customer uses an unsupported platform

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Customer opens the call link on an unsupported platform | System detects unsupported platform |
| 2 | 2. Observe the displayed message | User-friendly message advises to use a supported platform |

**Final Expected Result:** System informs the customer that the platform is unsupported and provides guidance.

---

### TC-346: Link access displays planned call time information

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Confirm that the call join page displays planned time details when opening a valid link.

**Preconditions:**
- A valid call link has been generated for a future scheduled call

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the valid call link in a supported browser | Call join page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Review the call information section | Planned date and time are displayed clearly |

**Final Expected Result:** The call join page shows the scheduled call time information.

---

### TC-347: Reject incoming call with predefined quick response sent within 2 seconds

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that a predefined quick response is sent within 2 seconds when rejecting an incoming call while online

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- At least one predefined quick response exists
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Select a predefined quick response message | Selected quick response is highlighted |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject and send message' | Call is rejected and message send is initiated |
| 4 | 4. Measure time from rejection to message sent status | Message is sent successfully within 2 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is rejected and the selected quick response is delivered within 2 seconds

---

### TC-348: Reject incoming call with predefined quick response when multiple options exist

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify correct selected quick response is sent when multiple predefined responses are available

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- Multiple predefined quick responses exist
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with multiple quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Select a specific predefined quick response (e.g., 'Kann gerade nicht sprechen') | Selected response is highlighted |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject and send message' | Call is rejected and message send is initiated |
| 4 | 4. Verify the sent message content in the conversation log | The exact selected quick response is shown as sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Correct selected quick response is sent after call rejection

---

### TC-349: Reject incoming call with no predefined quick responses available

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify standard message is sent when no predefined quick responses are available

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- No predefined quick responses exist
- Messaging service is available
- Standard message is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible without quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Reject' | Call is rejected and standard message send is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Verify the sent message content in the conversation log | Standard message is shown as sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is rejected and the standard message is sent

---

### TC-350: Reject incoming call when messaging service is temporarily unavailable

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify error handling when message delivery fails but call rejection succeeds

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- At least one predefined quick response exists
- Messaging service is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Select a predefined quick response | Selected response is highlighted |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject and send message' | Call is rejected and message send attempt is initiated |
| 4 | 4. Observe system feedback | Error message indicates message delivery failed |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is rejected and user receives an error indicating message delivery failure

---

### TC-351: Performance boundary: Message sent exactly at 2-second threshold

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify acceptance criteria boundary when message is sent at 2 seconds

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- At least one predefined quick response exists
- Messaging service is available with simulated 2-second latency

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Select a predefined quick response | Selected response is highlighted |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject and send message' | Call is rejected and message send is initiated |
| 4 | 4. Measure time from rejection to message sent status | Message is sent successfully at 2.0 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Message sent at the 2-second boundary is accepted as compliant

---

### TC-352: Negative: User attempts to reject without selecting quick response when options exist

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify system behavior when user tries to reject with quick response options but none selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- Multiple predefined quick responses exist
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Reject and send message' without selecting a quick response | System prompts user to select a quick response or defaults to standard behavior |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the prompt or default action | User can proceed with a selected message or standard message is used |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles missing selection gracefully without blocking call rejection

---

### TC-353: Negative: Messaging service recovers after failure and retry succeeds

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that retrying message send after a temporary outage succeeds

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is online
- Incoming call is displayed
- At least one predefined quick response exists
- Messaging service is initially unavailable then restored

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select a predefined quick response and reject the call while service is unavailable | Call is rejected and error message indicates message delivery failure |
| 2 | 2. Restore messaging service availability | Service status is available |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Retry send' from the error prompt | Message send is retried |
| 4 | 4. Verify the message status | Message is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** After recovery, retry sends the quick response successfully

---

### TC-354: Reject incoming call while offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify behavior when user is offline and rejects with quick response selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as primärer Nutzer
- User is offline
- Incoming call is displayed
- At least one predefined quick response exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen | Incoming call UI is visible with quick response options |
| 2 | 2. Select a predefined quick response | Selected response is highlighted |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject and send message' | Call is rejected and user is informed message cannot be sent while offline |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is rejected and user receives an offline delivery error or queued notification

---

### TC-355: Anrufverlauf anzeigen mit vorhandenen Einträgen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert die Anzeige des Anrufverlaufs mit gespeicherten Anrufen in korrekter chronologischer Reihenfolge inklusive Datum, Uhrzeit und Kontakt

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Nutzer hat Berechtigung auf den Anrufverlauf
- Es existieren gespeicherte Anrufe für den Nutzer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Anrufverlauf-Seite über die Navigation | Anrufverlauf-Seite wird geladen |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die Liste der Anrufe auf sichtbare Felder | Jeder Eintrag zeigt Datum, Uhrzeit und Kontakt |
| 3 | 3. Prüfe die Sortierung der Einträge | Einträge sind chronologisch sortiert (neuester zuerst oder wie spezifiziert) |

**Final Expected Result:** Anrufverlauf wird mit vollständigen Informationen in korrekter chronologischer Reihenfolge angezeigt

---

### TC-356: Leerer Anrufverlauf

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert die Anzeige einer leeren Ansicht mit Hinweis bei fehlenden gespeicherten Anrufen

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Nutzer hat Berechtigung auf den Anrufverlauf
- Es existieren keine gespeicherten Anrufe für den Nutzer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Anrufverlauf-Seite über die Navigation | Anrufverlauf-Seite wird geladen |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe den Inhalt der Seite | Leere Ansicht wird angezeigt |
| 3 | 3. Prüfe den Hinweistext | Hinweis erscheint, dass kein Anrufverlauf vorhanden ist |

**Final Expected Result:** Es wird eine leere Ansicht mit entsprechendem Hinweis angezeigt

---

### TC-357: Zugriff verweigert ohne Berechtigung

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Zugriff auf den Anrufverlauf ohne Berechtigung verweigert wird

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Nutzer hat keine Berechtigung auf den Anrufverlauf

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche die Anrufverlauf-Seite über die Navigation zu öffnen | Zugriff wird verweigert |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die Fehlermeldung | Eine entsprechende Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Zugriff wird verweigert und eine Fehlermeldung angezeigt

---

### TC-358: Chronologische Sortierung bei gleichen Daten

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert die Sortierung bei mehreren Einträgen am selben Datum mit unterschiedlichen Uhrzeiten

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Nutzer hat Berechtigung auf den Anrufverlauf
- Es existieren gespeicherte Anrufe mit gleichem Datum und unterschiedlichen Uhrzeiten

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Anrufverlauf-Seite | Anrufverlauf-Seite wird geladen |
| 2 | 2. Vergleiche die Reihenfolge der Einträge mit gleichem Datum | Einträge sind nach Uhrzeit korrekt sortiert |

**Final Expected Result:** Einträge am gleichen Datum sind korrekt nach Uhrzeit sortiert

---

### TC-359: Grenzfall: Sehr viele Anrufe

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert die Stabilität und korrekte Anzeige bei einer großen Anzahl von Anrufen

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Nutzer hat Berechtigung auf den Anrufverlauf
- Es existieren sehr viele gespeicherte Anrufe (z. B. 1000+)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Anrufverlauf-Seite | Anrufverlauf-Seite wird geladen |
| 2 | 2. Scrolle durch die Liste bis zum Ende | Liste bleibt stabil und vollständig, keine fehlenden Einträge |

**Final Expected Result:** Anrufverlauf wird auch bei vielen Einträgen korrekt und stabil angezeigt

---

### TC-360: Nicht angemeldeter Nutzer versucht Anrufverlauf zu öffnen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass nicht angemeldete Nutzer den Anrufverlauf nicht einsehen können

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist nicht angemeldet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Versuche die Anrufverlauf-Seite direkt über URL aufzurufen | Zugriff wird blockiert |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die Weiterleitung oder Meldung | Nutzer wird zur Anmeldung aufgefordert oder erhält eine Fehlermeldung |

**Final Expected Result:** Nicht angemeldete Nutzer erhalten keinen Zugriff auf den Anrufverlauf

---

### TC-361: Publish status with valid content

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can publish a status with content and it is visible for 24 hours

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection
- User has at least one contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid text content into the status input field | Text appears in the input field |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing process starts and completes successfully |
| 4 | 4. View the status list as the user | New status is visible in the list |
| 5 | 5. View the status as a contact | Status is visible to contacts |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved and visible to contacts for 24 hours

---

### TC-362: Prevent publishing empty status

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify publishing is blocked when no content is provided

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Ensure the status input field is empty | No content is present |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing is prevented |

**Final Expected Result:** A clear error message is shown and no status is created

---

### TC-363: Handle internet disconnect during publishing

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify error handling and retry option when connectivity drops during publish

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Internet connection is initially available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid text content | Text appears in the input field |
| 3 | 3. Disable internet connection | Device is offline |
| 4 | 4. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing fails with an error |
| 5 | 5. Re-enable internet connection and tap retry | Publishing resumes and completes |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows an error and allows retry; on retry with internet, status publishes successfully

---

### TC-364: Boundary: publish status with maximum allowed content length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify publishing succeeds when content is at maximum allowed length

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection
- Maximum content length is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content exactly at the maximum allowed length | Input accepts the full content without truncation |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing process completes successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved and visible to contacts for 24 hours

---

### TC-365: Boundary: publish status with one character

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify minimal non-empty content can be published

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a single character | Single character is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing process completes successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved and visible to contacts for 24 hours

---

### TC-366: Prevent publishing whitespace-only status

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify publishing is blocked when content is only whitespace

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter only spaces in the input field | Spaces are shown in the input field |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing is prevented |

**Final Expected Result:** A clear error message is shown and no status is created

---

### TC-367: Status visibility expires after 24 hours

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify status is no longer visible to contacts after 24 hours

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection
- User has at least one contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Publish a valid status | Status is published and visible to contacts |
| 2 | 2. Advance system time by 24 hours (or wait 24 hours in test environment) | 24 hours have elapsed since publish time |
| 3 | 3. View the status list as a contact | Status is no longer visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Status expires and is not visible after 24 hours

---

### TC-368: Ensure status not visible to contacts before publish confirmation

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify status is not visible to contacts if publishing fails

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Internet connection is unstable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid content | Content is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Simulate network failure and tap "Veröffentlichen" | Error is shown and publish fails |
| 4 | 4. View status list as a contact | No new status is visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Unpublished status is not visible to contacts

---

### TC-369: Publish status with special characters and emojis

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify content with special characters and emojis can be published

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Status creation screen | Status editor is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content containing emojis and special characters | Input displays characters correctly |
| 3 | 3. Tap on "Veröffentlichen" | Publishing completes successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved with correct characters and visible for 24 hours

---

### TC-370: TC01 - Anzeige des Kontaktstatus beim Öffnen aus der Kontaktliste

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass beim Öffnen eines Kontakts aus der Kontaktliste der aktuelle Status klar angezeigt wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontakt hat einen gültigen Status (z.B. 'Verfügbar')

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Kontaktliste | Kontaktliste wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Klicke auf einen Kontakt mit Status 'Verfügbar' | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Der aktuelle Status 'Verfügbar' wird klar und eindeutig angezeigt.

---

### TC-371: TC02 - Anzeige des Kontaktstatus beim Öffnen im Chat

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass beim Öffnen eines Kontakts im Chat der Status sichtbar ist.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontakt hat einen gültigen Status (z.B. 'Beschäftigt')

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne einen bestehenden Chat mit einem Kontakt | Chat-Ansicht wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Beobachte den Statusbereich des Kontakts | Statusbereich ist sichtbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Status 'Beschäftigt' wird klar und eindeutig angezeigt.

---

### TC-372: TC03 - Neutraler Statusindikator bei fehlendem Status

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein neutraler Status oder der Hinweis 'Status nicht verfügbar' angezeigt wird, wenn kein Status vorhanden ist.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontakt hat keinen verfügbaren oder freigegebenen Status

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Kontaktliste | Kontaktliste wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Klicke auf den Kontakt ohne Status | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Ein neutraler Statusindikator oder der Hinweis 'Status nicht verfügbar' wird angezeigt.

---

### TC-373: TC04 - Fehleranzeige bei fehlender Netzwerkverbindung

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass bei fehlender Netzwerkverbindung ein Fehler angezeigt und der zuletzt bekannte Status beibehalten wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontakt hat einen zuvor bekannten Status

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trenne die Netzwerkverbindung des Geräts | Gerät hat keine Netzwerkverbindung |
| 2 | 2. Öffne einen Kontakt aus der Kontaktliste | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Das System zeigt eine Fehlermeldung an und behält den zuletzt bekannten Status bei.

---

### TC-374: TC05 - Fehleranzeige bei nicht erreichbarem Statusdienst

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass bei Ausfall des Statusdienstes eine Fehlermeldung angezeigt und der zuletzt bekannte Status beibehalten wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Statusdienst ist nicht erreichbar
- Kontakt hat einen zuvor bekannten Status

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simuliere den Ausfall des Statusdienstes | Statusdienst antwortet nicht |
| 2 | 2. Öffne einen Kontakt aus der Kontaktliste | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Das System informiert über den Fehler und zeigt den zuletzt bekannten Status an.

---

### TC-375: TC06 - Aktualisierung des Status bei wiederhergestellter Verbindung

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Status nach Wiederherstellung der Verbindung aktualisiert wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontaktstatus hat sich zwischenzeitlich geändert
- Netzwerkverbindung war unterbrochen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Stelle die Netzwerkverbindung wieder her | Gerät ist online |
| 2 | 2. Öffne den Kontakt erneut oder aktualisiere die Ansicht | Statusabruf wird initiiert |

**Final Expected Result:** Der aktuelle Kontaktstatus wird aktualisiert und angezeigt.

---

### TC-376: TC07 - Anzeige des Status bei schnellem Wechsel zwischen Kontakten

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass beim schnellen Wechsel zwischen Kontakten die jeweils korrekten Status angezeigt werden.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Mehrere Kontakte mit unterschiedlichen Status vorhanden

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne Kontakt A | Kontaktdetails oder Chat von Kontakt A wird geöffnet |
| 2 | 2. Wechsle sofort zu Kontakt B | Kontaktdetails oder Chat von Kontakt B wird geöffnet |
| 3 | 3. Wechsle sofort zu Kontakt C | Kontaktdetails oder Chat von Kontakt C wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Für jeden Kontakt wird der jeweils korrekte Status angezeigt, ohne Vermischung.

---

### TC-377: TC08 - Statusanzeige bei leeren Kontaktlisten (Boundary)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass keine Statusanzeige fehlschlägt, wenn die Kontaktliste leer ist.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist leer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Kontaktliste | Leere Kontaktliste wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Kein Status wird angezeigt und keine Fehlermeldung tritt auf.

---

### TC-378: TC09 - Statusanzeige für Kontakt mit Sonderzeichen im Namen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass die Statusanzeige korrekt funktioniert, auch wenn der Kontaktname Sonderzeichen enthält.

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontaktname enthält Sonderzeichen
- Kontakt hat einen gültigen Status

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Kontaktliste | Kontaktliste wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Klicke auf den Kontakt mit Sonderzeichen im Namen | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |

**Final Expected Result:** Der aktuelle Status wird korrekt angezeigt.

---

### TC-379: TC10 - UI-Klarheit der Statusanzeige (Lesbarkeit)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass der Status visuell klar und eindeutig erkennbar ist (z.B. Text + Symbol).

**Preconditions:**
- Endnutzer ist angemeldet
- Kontaktliste ist geladen
- Kontakt hat einen gültigen Status

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne einen Kontakt in der Liste | Kontaktdetails oder Chat wird geöffnet |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die Darstellung des Status (Text/Symbol/Farbe) | Statusanzeige ist sichtbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Status ist klar, eindeutig und gut lesbar dargestellt.

---

### TC-380: Send Status-Antwort within allowed window

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Verify that a status reply can be sent and displayed as a status reply when the incoming status message is within the allowed response window.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message within the allowed response window
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the incoming status message | Chat opens and the incoming status message is visible |
| 2 | 2. Select the status message and choose the 'Status-Antwort' action | Status-Antwort input UI is shown |
| 3 | 3. Enter a valid response and click 'Send' | Send action is initiated without validation errors |
| 4 | 4. Observe the chat timeline | The response appears in the chat and is marked as a Status-Antwort |

**Final Expected Result:** Status-Antwort is delivered and correctly labeled in the chat timeline

---

### TC-381: Prevent Status-Antwort when response window expired

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Ensure sending a status reply is blocked when the status message is older than the allowed response window.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message older than the allowed response window
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the expired status message | Chat opens and the expired status message is visible |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to send a Status-Antwort to the expired message | Sending is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Review the UI feedback | A clear, understandable message indicates the response window has expired |

**Final Expected Result:** Status-Antwort is not sent and user receives an understandable error message

---

### TC-382: Show error and retry option when connection drops during send

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Verify error handling and retry option when network connection is interrupted during sending.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message within the allowed response window
- Network connection can be interrupted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat and initiate a Status-Antwort | Status-Antwort input UI is shown |
| 2 | 2. Enter a response and click 'Send' | Send action is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Interrupt the network connection during sending | Send fails due to connection loss |
| 4 | 4. Observe the UI | A failure message is displayed and a retry option is offered |
| 5 | 5. Restore network and click 'Retry' | Message is sent successfully |
| 6 | 6. Check chat timeline | The response appears and is labeled as Status-Antwort |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows error and provides retry; on retry the Status-Antwort is delivered and labeled

---

### TC-383: Boundary: response window just before expiry

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Ensure a status reply can be sent at the last moment before the response window expires.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message with remaining response time of 1 second
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat and select the status message | Status-Antwort input UI is shown |
| 2 | 2. Immediately enter a response and click 'Send' before expiration | Send action is accepted |
| 3 | 3. Observe the chat timeline | The response is delivered and labeled as Status-Antwort |

**Final Expected Result:** Status-Antwort is delivered successfully at the boundary before expiry

---

### TC-384: Boundary: response window just after expiry

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Ensure a status reply is blocked immediately after the response window expires.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message that expired 1 second ago
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat and attempt to send a Status-Antwort | Send action is prevented |
| 2 | 2. Review the UI feedback | An understandable message indicates the response window has expired |

**Final Expected Result:** Status-Antwort is blocked and a clear error message is shown

---

### TC-385: Status-Antwort labeling in chat history

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Validate that the sent response is correctly labeled as a Status-Antwort in the chat history.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message within the allowed response window
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a Status-Antwort to the incoming status message | Message is sent successfully |
| 2 | 2. Scroll the chat timeline to the sent message | The sent message is visible |
| 3 | 3. Verify the message metadata/label | Message is clearly marked as Status-Antwort |

**Final Expected Result:** Status-Antwort is labeled correctly in the chat history

---

### TC-386: Do not allow send when no status message is selected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Ensure the system prevents sending a Status-Antwort if no incoming status message is selected.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat has no selected incoming status message
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat without selecting a status message | Chat opens with no status message selected |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to access Status-Antwort send action | Action is disabled or a prompt requests selecting a status message |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents sending and guides the user to select a status message

---

### TC-387: Retry option visible only on send failure

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-044
**Requirement:** WA-STS-003

**Description:** Verify retry option appears only after a send failure due to connection interruption.

**Preconditions:**
- Kundenservice-Mitarbeiter is logged in
- A chat contains an incoming status message within the allowed response window
- Network connection is stable initially

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a Status-Antwort with stable connection | No retry option is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Interrupt the connection during sending | Send fails |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI | Retry option is displayed after failure |

**Final Expected Result:** Retry option appears only after a send failure

---

### TC-388: Set Status-Sichtbarkeit auf „Niemand“

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify that setting status visibility to 'Niemand' hides status from all other users.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page
- Another user exists who is not a contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the Status-Sichtbarkeit option 'Niemand'. | Option 'Niemand' is selected. |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Speichern'. | Save action completes successfully and a confirmation is shown. |
| 3 | 3. As another user, view the user's status. | Status is not visible to the other user. |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is hidden from all other users after saving 'Niemand'.

---

### TC-389: Set Status-Sichtbarkeit auf „Nur Kontakte“

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify that status visibility set to 'Nur Kontakte' is visible only to contacts.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page
- One contact user exists
- One non-contact user exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the Status-Sichtbarkeit option 'Nur Kontakte'. | Option 'Nur Kontakte' is selected. |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Speichern'. | Save action completes successfully and a confirmation is shown. |
| 3 | 3. As a contact user, view the user's status. | Status is visible to the contact user. |
| 4 | 4. As a non-contact user, view the user's status. | Status is not visible to the non-contact user. |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is visible only to contacts after saving 'Nur Kontakte'.

---

### TC-390: Save Status-Sichtbarkeit without network connection

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify error handling when saving visibility settings with no network connection.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page
- Current status visibility is set to 'Nur Kontakte'

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Disable network connectivity on the device. | Device shows no network connection. |
| 2 | 2. Change Status-Sichtbarkeit to 'Niemand'. | Option 'Niemand' is selected. |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Speichern'. | An error message is displayed indicating save failed due to no network. |
| 4 | 4. Re-enable network and reload settings. | Previously saved setting 'Nur Kontakte' is still displayed. |

**Final Expected Result:** Save fails with error and previous setting remains unchanged.

---

### TC-391: Verify persistence of saved visibility setting after page reload

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Ensure saved visibility setting persists after reloading the Datenschutzeinstellungen page.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Set Status-Sichtbarkeit to 'Niemand' and click 'Speichern'. | Save action completes successfully and a confirmation is shown. |
| 2 | 2. Reload the Datenschutzeinstellungen page. | The selected option remains 'Niemand'. |

**Final Expected Result:** Saved visibility setting persists after page reload.

---

### TC-392: Attempt to save without changing selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify behavior when user clicks save without changing the current visibility setting.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page
- Current status visibility is set to 'Nur Kontakte'

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Without changing the selection, click 'Speichern'. | Save action completes successfully or is no-op with an informational message. |
| 2 | 2. Reload the Datenschutzeinstellungen page. | The selected option remains 'Nur Kontakte'. |

**Final Expected Result:** No change occurs and the existing setting remains intact.

---

### TC-393: Switch visibility between options multiple times before saving

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify that the last selected option is the one saved.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Datenschutzeinstellungen page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select 'Niemand'. | Option 'Niemand' is selected. |
| 2 | 2. Select 'Nur Kontakte'. | Option 'Nur Kontakte' is selected. |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Speichern'. | Save action completes successfully and a confirmation is shown. |
| 4 | 4. Reload the Datenschutzeinstellungen page. | The selected option remains 'Nur Kontakte'. |

**Final Expected Result:** The last selected option before saving is persisted.

---

### TC-394: Unauthorized access to privacy settings page

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-045
**Requirement:** WA-STS-004

**Description:** Verify that an unauthenticated user cannot access Datenschutzeinstellungen.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged out

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate directly to the Datenschutzeinstellungen URL. | User is redirected to login or shown an access denied message. |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthenticated users cannot access privacy settings.

---

### TC-395: Mute status notifications for an active contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify that a user can mute status notifications for an active contact and the mute indicator is shown

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and has status notifications enabled
- User is in the contact view of the active contact
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact options menu in the contact view | Options menu is displayed with the item “Status stumm schalten” |
| 2 | 2. Select “Status stumm schalten” | System processes the mute request without error |
| 3 | 3. Observe the contact view after the action | Mute indicator is visible for the contact |
| 4 | 4. Trigger a status update from the contact (e.g., simulated status post) | No status notification is shown for this contact |

**Final Expected Result:** Status notifications for the contact are muted and the mute status is visibly set

---

### TC-396: Attempt to mute an already muted contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify the system indicates the contact is already muted and does not change state

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and already muted
- User is in the contact view of the muted contact
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact options menu in the contact view | Options menu is displayed with the item “Status stumm schalten” |
| 2 | 2. Select “Status stumm schalten” | System shows message indicating the contact is already muted |
| 3 | 3. Observe the mute indicator | Mute indicator remains visible with no change |

**Final Expected Result:** System informs the user that the contact is already muted and the mute state remains unchanged

---

### TC-397: Mute status fails due to network interruption

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify error handling when network is down while muting a contact

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and not muted
- User is in the contact view of the contact
- Network connection is interrupted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact options menu in the contact view | Options menu is displayed with the item “Status stumm schalten” |
| 2 | 2. Select “Status stumm schalten” | System attempts to save and detects network interruption |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | Error message is displayed indicating the operation failed |
| 4 | 4. Check the mute indicator | Mute indicator is not set; status remains unmuted |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives an error and the mute status remains unchanged

---

### TC-398: Mute status fails due to server-side save error

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify error handling when backend save fails during muting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and not muted
- User is in the contact view of the contact
- Network connection is stable
- Backend is configured to return a save error

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact options menu in the contact view | Options menu is displayed with the item “Status stumm schalten” |
| 2 | 2. Select “Status stumm schalten” | System sends mute request to backend |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | Error message is displayed due to save failure |
| 4 | 4. Check the mute indicator | Mute indicator is not set; status remains unmuted |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives an error and the mute status remains unchanged

---

### TC-399: Mute status option visibility for inactive contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify behavior when attempting to mute a contact that is not active

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is inactive
- User is in the contact view of the inactive contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact options menu in the contact view | Options menu is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Check for the “Status stumm schalten” option | Option is hidden or disabled for inactive contact |

**Final Expected Result:** User cannot mute status for an inactive contact

---

### TC-400: Mute status persistence after app restart

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify that mute status remains after closing and reopening the app

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and muted
- Mute indicator is visible before restart

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Close the application | Application is closed |
| 2 | 2. Reopen the application and navigate to the same contact view | Contact view is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Observe the mute indicator | Mute indicator is still visible |
| 4 | 4. Trigger a status update from the contact | No status notification is shown for this contact |

**Final Expected Result:** Mute status persists across app restart and notifications remain suppressed

---

### TC-401: Concurrent mute request from two sessions

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify idempotent behavior when the same contact is muted from another session

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on two sessions (A and B)
- Contact is active and not muted
- Both sessions are in the contact view
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. In session A, select “Status stumm schalten” | Mute request succeeds and indicator is shown in session A |
| 2 | 2. In session B, select “Status stumm schalten” shortly after | System indicates contact is already muted |
| 3 | 3. Refresh contact view in session B | Mute indicator is visible and consistent with session A |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles concurrent requests correctly and keeps mute state consistent

---

### TC-402: UI feedback timing on mute action

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-046
**Requirement:** WA-STS-005

**Description:** Verify that user receives timely UI feedback during muting

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Contact is active and not muted
- User is in the contact view
- Network connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select “Status stumm schalten” | UI shows loading indicator or immediate feedback within acceptable time (e.g., 2 seconds) |
| 2 | 2. Wait for the operation to complete | Mute indicator is shown without excessive delay |

**Final Expected Result:** Mute action provides timely feedback and completes within acceptable performance limits

---

### TC-403: Send supported image successfully in existing chat

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify that a logged-in user can send a supported image and it appears for both sender and recipient

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User B is logged in
- A and B have an existing chat
- User A is viewing the chat with User B
- Network connection is stable
- Image file is supported (e.g., JPG) and within size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tap the image attachment icon in the chat composer | Image picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid JPG image within size limit | Image is attached and previewed in the composer |
| 3 | 3. Tap the “Senden” button | Message is sent and appears in User A's chat view |
| 4 | 4. Verify User B's chat view | Image message appears for User B |

**Final Expected Result:** Supported image is sent successfully and displayed for both sender and recipient

---

### TC-404: Prevent sending unsupported file format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify unsupported file formats cannot be sent and a clear error message is shown

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Network connection is stable
- Unsupported file format is available (e.g., .exe or .txt)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tap the image attachment icon | Image picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a file with an unsupported format | System prevents selection or flags the file as invalid |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send the selected file (if selection was allowed) | Send is blocked and a clear error message about unsupported format is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Unsupported formats are not sent and a understandable error is shown

---

### TC-405: Reject image larger than maximum size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify that images exceeding the maximum allowed size are rejected with a size limit message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Network connection is stable
- Image file exceeds maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tap the image attachment icon | Image picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select an image larger than the maximum size limit | System allows selection but validates size |
| 3 | 3. Tap the “Senden” button | Sending is rejected and a message indicates the size limit |

**Final Expected Result:** Oversized images are not sent and size limit warning is displayed

---

### TC-406: Allow image at maximum size boundary

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify that an image exactly at the maximum size limit can be sent

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Network connection is stable
- Image file is supported and exactly at max size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tap the image attachment icon | Image picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select an image exactly at the maximum size limit | Image is attached and previewed |
| 3 | 3. Tap the “Senden” button | Image is sent successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Image at size boundary is sent and displayed in chat

---

### TC-407: Handle network failure during image upload

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify that an upload failure due to unstable network shows an error and allows retry

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Network connection is unstable or simulated to fail during upload
- Image file is supported and within size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach a valid image | Image is attached and previewed |
| 2 | 2. Tap the “Senden” button while network failure occurs | Upload fails and an error message is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Tap the retry option | System attempts to resend the image |

**Final Expected Result:** User is informed of failure and can retry sending the image

---

### TC-408: Retry succeeds after network recovery

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify that retrying after network recovery sends the image successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Image upload previously failed due to network error
- Network is restored

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. From the failed image message, tap the retry option | Retry is initiated |
| 2 | 2. Observe upload status | Upload completes successfully |
| 3 | 3. Verify recipient view | Image appears in recipient's chat |

**Final Expected Result:** Retry sends the image successfully after network recovery

---

### TC-409: Display error for unsupported image format with clear message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify error message content is understandable for unsupported formats

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an existing chat
- Unsupported format file is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to attach an unsupported file format | System blocks or flags the file |
| 2 | 2. Observe the error message | Message explicitly states that the format is not supported |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives a clear, understandable unsupported format error

---

### TC-410: Prevent sending when not in an existing chat

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-047
**Requirement:** WA-MED-001

**Description:** Verify sending is not possible if the user is not in an existing chat (guard condition)

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is not inside an existing chat view

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a non-chat screen (e.g., chat list) and attempt to send an image | No send option available or user is prompted to open a chat |

**Final Expected Result:** Image sending is only possible within an existing chat

---

### TC-411: Send supported video successfully with preview

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify a supported video can be uploaded and delivered with preview in an open conversation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- Supported video format is available on device

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the attachment icon in the message composer | Attachment options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a supported video file within the size limit | Selected video is queued for upload |
| 3 | 3. Click the send button | Upload starts and progress is shown |
| 4 | 4. Wait for upload to complete | Video message appears in the conversation with a preview thumbnail |

**Final Expected Result:** Video is uploaded and delivered as a message with preview

---

### TC-412: Block sending video exceeding maximum size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify sending a video larger than the maximum size is blocked with a clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- Video file exceeds maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the attachment icon in the message composer | Attachment options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a video file that exceeds the maximum size limit | System validates the file size |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send the video | Sending is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees a clear error message indicating the maximum allowed size and the message is not sent

---

### TC-413: Handle network interruption during video upload

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify upload failure is indicated and a retry option is offered when network disconnects

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- Supported video format is available on device
- Network can be interrupted during upload

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select a supported video file within the size limit | Selected video is queued for upload |
| 2 | 2. Click the send button to start upload | Upload starts and progress is shown |
| 3 | 3. Disconnect network during upload | Upload is interrupted |
