| 4 | 4. Observe the message state | Message is marked as failed and a retry option is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Failed upload is indicated and user can retry the upload

---

### TC-414: Retry upload after network interruption

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify retrying a failed upload succeeds after network is restored

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- A video upload previously failed due to network interruption
- Network connectivity is restored

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the retry option on the failed video message | Upload restarts and progress is shown |
| 2 | 2. Wait for upload to complete | Video message appears with preview thumbnail |

**Final Expected Result:** Video upload completes successfully after retry and is delivered with preview

---

### TC-415: Send video at maximum size boundary

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify a video exactly at the maximum size limit can be sent successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- Video file is exactly at the maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the video file at the size limit | Selected video is queued for upload |
| 2 | 2. Click the send button | Upload starts and progress is shown |
| 3 | 3. Wait for upload to complete | Video message appears with preview thumbnail |

**Final Expected Result:** Video at maximum allowed size is uploaded and delivered with preview

---

### TC-416: Attempt to send unsupported video format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-048
**Requirement:** WA-MED-002

**Description:** Verify system prevents sending unsupported video formats and shows a clear message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing conversation open
- Unsupported video format file is available on device

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the attachment icon in the message composer | Attachment options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an unsupported video file format | System validates file format |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send the video | Sending is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees a clear error message indicating unsupported format and the message is not sent

---

### TC-417: Successful send of supported document

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Verify that a supported document can be sent and confirmation is shown

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected
- User has a supported document available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the document send screen | Send screen is displayed with recipient details |
| 2 | 2. Select a supported document (e.g., PDF) | Selected document appears in the upload list |
| 3 | 3. Click on "Senden" | Upload starts and progress indicator is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Document is successfully transferred and a shipping/transfer confirmation is displayed

---

### TC-418: Block sending of oversized document

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Ensure large documents beyond size limit are blocked with a clear message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected
- User has a document larger than the maximum allowed size

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the document send screen | Send screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an oversized document | System detects size and shows size limitation message |
| 3 | 3. Click on "Senden" | Sending is prevented and the document is not uploaded |

**Final Expected Result:** User is informed about size restrictions and sending is blocked

---

### TC-419: Network interruption during sending

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Verify error handling and retry option when the network disconnects during sending

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected
- User has a supported document
- Network can be toggled for test

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start sending a supported document | Upload starts and progress indicator is shown |
| 2 | 2. Simulate network disconnection during upload | Upload halts and an error message is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Reconnect network and click "Erneut versuchen" | Upload resumes or restarts and progress indicator is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives an error message and can retry sending after reconnecting

---

### TC-420: Boundary test: document size exactly at maximum limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Ensure a document at the maximum size limit is allowed and sent successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected
- User has a document exactly at the size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select a document equal to the maximum size limit | Document is accepted for upload |
| 2 | 2. Click on "Senden" | Upload starts without size error |

**Final Expected Result:** Document at the size limit is sent successfully and confirmation is shown

---

### TC-421: Negative: unsupported document type

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Ensure unsupported file types are rejected with an appropriate message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected
- User has an unsupported document type

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to select an unsupported document type (e.g., .exe) | System shows a message that the file type is not supported |
| 2 | 2. Click on "Senden" | Sending is prevented |

**Final Expected Result:** Unsupported file types cannot be sent and user is informed

---

### TC-422: Negative: send without selecting a document

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Verify that sending without a document is not allowed

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Recipient is selected

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the document send screen | Send screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on "Senden" without selecting a document | Validation message prompts user to select a document |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents sending and displays a validation message

---

### TC-423: Negative: send without selecting a recipient

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-049
**Requirement:** WA-MED-003

**Description:** Ensure that sending is blocked if no recipient is selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- No recipient is selected
- User has a supported document

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the document send screen | Send screen is displayed without recipient details |
| 2 | 2. Select a supported document | Selected document appears in the upload list |
| 3 | 3. Click on "Senden" | System prompts user to select a recipient |

**Final Expected Result:** Sending is blocked until a recipient is selected

### TC-424: Crop and rotate image with preview and send

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that a user can crop and rotate an image, see a preview, and send the edited image

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Crop the image to a smaller area | Crop area is applied and preview updates to the cropped view |
| 3 | 3. Rotate the image by 90 degrees | Preview updates to show the rotated, cropped image |
| 4 | 4. Confirm the edit and send the message | Message is sent with the edited image |

**Final Expected Result:** Edited (cropped and rotated) image is sent and matches the preview

---

### TC-425: Send original image without edits

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that confirming without edits sends the original image unchanged

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Make no edits and confirm | Edit confirmation completes without changes |
| 3 | 3. Send the message | Message is sent with the original image |

**Final Expected Result:** Original image is sent without any modifications

---

### TC-426: Handle edit failure during crop

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that a failed edit action shows an error and preserves the original image

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open
- Simulated edit failure is possible (e.g., storage/write error)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to crop the image while forcing an edit failure | An error message is displayed indicating the edit failed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm sending the image | Message is sent with the original, unedited image |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees an error and the original image remains unchanged

---

### TC-427: Cancel editing preserves original image

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that canceling the editing action keeps the original image unchanged

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Apply a crop and rotation | Preview updates to show the edited image |
| 3 | 3. Cancel the editing operation | Editing interface closes and original image remains selected |
| 4 | 4. Send the message | Message is sent with the original image |

**Final Expected Result:** Canceling discards edits and the original image is sent

---

### TC-428: Boundary: Crop to minimum allowed size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify cropping to the minimum allowed size is accepted and preview updates correctly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Resize crop box to the minimum allowed dimensions | Crop box respects minimum size constraints |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the crop and send | Preview shows the minimally cropped image and message sends |

**Final Expected Result:** Image is sent with the minimum-size crop applied

---

### TC-429: Boundary: Rotate image full 360 degrees

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify rotating 360 degrees results in the original orientation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Rotate the image four times by 90 degrees | Preview returns to the original orientation |
| 3 | 3. Confirm edits and send | Message is sent with image in original orientation |

**Final Expected Result:** A 360-degree rotation yields the original orientation and is sent correctly

---

### TC-430: Preview reflects combined crop and rotate edits

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that the preview always matches the combined edit operations before sending

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Crop the image to a new area | Preview updates to the cropped area |
| 3 | 3. Rotate the image by 180 degrees | Preview shows the cropped image rotated 180 degrees |
| 4 | 4. Confirm and send | Message is sent with the image matching the preview |

**Final Expected Result:** Sent image matches the preview after combined edits

---

### TC-431: Error message when rotation fails

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that a failed rotation action shows an error and preserves the original image

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open
- Simulated rotation failure is possible (e.g., processing error)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to rotate the image while forcing a failure | An error message is displayed indicating the rotation failed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm sending the image | Message is sent with the original, unedited image |

**Final Expected Result:** User is notified of the failure and the original image is unchanged

---

### TC-432: Integration: Edited image payload is sent

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-050
**Requirement:** WA-MED-004

**Description:** Verify that the backend receives the edited image data when edits are applied

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an image available to attach
- Message composer is open
- Backend request inspection is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an image to the message | Image opens in the editing interface |
| 2 | 2. Apply crop and rotation edits | Preview updates to show edited image |
| 3 | 3. Confirm and send the message | Message send request is issued to the backend |
| 4 | 4. Inspect the outgoing request payload | Payload contains edited image data (not the original) |

**Final Expected Result:** Backend receives the edited image data when edits are applied

---

### TC-433: Send sticker successfully in active chat

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify that a selected sticker is sent and displayed for both sender and receiver

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog is available
- Recipient is available to receive messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker in the chat | Sticker picker opens and displays available stickers |
| 2 | 2. Select a sticker | Selected sticker is highlighted/previewed |
| 3 | 3. Tap the Send button | Sticker is sent from the client |
| 4 | 4. Observe the chat timeline for the sender | Sticker appears in the sender chat timeline |
| 5 | 5. Observe the recipient chat timeline | Sticker appears in the recipient chat timeline |

**Final Expected Result:** Selected sticker is displayed in the chat for both sender and recipient

---

### TC-434: Sticker picker loads without noticeable delay

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify that sticker picker loads quickly in an active chat

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog is available
- Stable network connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker in the chat | Sticker picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Measure time until stickers are fully rendered | Stickers render within acceptable threshold (e.g., <= 500ms) |

**Final Expected Result:** Sticker picker loads and displays stickers without noticeable delay

---

### TC-435: Sticker picker shows empty state when no stickers are available

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify empty state message and sending is disabled when sticker list is empty

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog is empty

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker in the chat | Empty state view is displayed with a hint message |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to send a sticker | Send action is disabled or unavailable |

**Final Expected Result:** Empty state is shown and sending a sticker is not possible

---

### TC-436: Handle sticker send failure due to connection loss

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify error handling and retry when send fails

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker and select a sticker | Sticker is selected |
| 2 | 2. Disable network connection | Network is unavailable |
| 3 | 3. Tap the Send button | Send attempt fails |
| 4 | 4. Observe the UI feedback | Error message is shown with option to retry |
| 5 | 5. Re-enable network connection | Network is available |
| 6 | 6. Tap retry | Sticker send is retried |

**Final Expected Result:** User is notified of failure and can successfully retry sending the sticker

---

### TC-437: Prevent sending without selecting a sticker

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify that send is not possible when no sticker is selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker | Sticker picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to tap Send without selecting a sticker | Send action is disabled or no message is sent |

**Final Expected Result:** Sticker cannot be sent without a selection

---

### TC-438: Sticker picker displays correctly with large catalog

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-051
**Requirement:** WA-MED-005

**Description:** Verify boundary behavior when many stickers are available

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Sticker catalog contains a large number of stickers

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the sticker picker | Sticker picker opens and shows stickers |
| 2 | 2. Scroll through sticker list | Stickers load and render correctly without UI issues |
| 3 | 3. Select a sticker from the end of the list | Sticker is selectable and highlighted |

**Final Expected Result:** Sticker picker remains usable with a large catalog and selection works

---

### TC-439: Successful GIF search and insert into message

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that a user can search for a GIF and insert it into the message composer with stable internet.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in message composer
- Stable internet connection
- GIF service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the GIF search from the message composer | GIF search UI is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid keyword (e.g., 'happy') and submit the search | Relevant GIF results are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Select a GIF from the results | Selected GIF is inserted into the message composer |

**Final Expected Result:** The selected GIF appears in the message composer ready to send

---

### TC-440: Send message with inserted GIF successfully

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that a message containing an inserted GIF is sent and delivered reliably.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in message composer
- Stable internet connection
- GIF already inserted in composer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the Send button | Message is sent without errors |
| 2 | 2. Observe the conversation thread | Sent message appears with the GIF correctly rendered |

**Final Expected Result:** GIF message is delivered and visible in the conversation

---

### TC-441: No results returned for GIF search

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that a helpful message is shown when GIF search yields no results and no GIF is inserted.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in GIF search UI
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a rare/invalid keyword (e.g., 'asdlkjasd123') and submit | No GIF results are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Observe the UI feedback | A clear 'no results' message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** No GIF is inserted and a user-friendly no-results message is shown

---

### TC-442: GIF search fails due to service error

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that a service error during GIF search shows an error and allows retry.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in message composer
- GIF service returns 5xx error

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open GIF search and enter a valid keyword | Search request is triggered |
| 2 | 2. Simulate a service error response | An error message is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Click the retry action | Search is reattempted |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees an error message and can retry GIF search

---

### TC-443: GIF search fails due to no internet connection

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that lack of connectivity shows an error and allows retry.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in message composer
- Internet connection is disabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open GIF search and enter a valid keyword | Search request fails due to no connection |
| 2 | 2. Observe the UI feedback | A connectivity error message is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Re-enable internet and click retry | GIF results load successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Connectivity error is shown and retry succeeds after restoring connection

---

### TC-444: Boundary: Empty search keyword

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify behavior when the search keyword is empty.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in GIF search UI
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave the search field empty | Search field remains empty |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit search | Search is not performed and a validation message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** User is prompted to enter a keyword and no GIFs are inserted

---

### TC-445: Boundary: Very long search keyword

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify handling of excessively long keywords.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in GIF search UI
- Stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a very long keyword (e.g., 256+ characters) | Input accepts the text or trims to maximum length |
| 2 | 2. Submit the search | Search executes without UI crash; results or no-results message are shown |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles long input gracefully without inserting unintended GIFs

---

### TC-446: Select and replace a GIF in composer

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify that selecting a different GIF replaces the previous selection before sending.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in message composer
- Stable internet connection
- GIF search is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Search and select a first GIF | First GIF is inserted into the composer |
| 2 | 2. Search again and select a second GIF | Second GIF replaces or adds according to design without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Composer reflects the latest intended GIF selection with correct behavior

---

### TC-447: Performance: GIF search response time under stable internet

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-052
**Requirement:** WA-MED-006

**Description:** Verify GIF search results load within acceptable performance threshold.

- User is in GIF search UI
- Stable internet connection
- Service SLA defined (e.g., <2s)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a common keyword and submit search | Search request is triggered |
| 2 | 2. Measure time until results are displayed | Results load within the defined SLA |

**Final Expected Result:** GIF search meets performance expectations

---

### TC-448: Kamera-Foto aufnehmen und Vorschau im Chat anzeigen

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein aufgenommenes Foto im Chat als Vorschau angezeigt und sendbar ist, wenn die Berechtigung erteilt ist.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Kamera ist verfügbar und nicht durch andere Apps belegt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Die Kamera-Ansicht öffnet sich |
| 2 | 2. Nimm ein Foto auf | Ein Foto wird aufgenommen und gespeichert |
| 3 | 3. Bestätige das Foto zur Verwendung im Chat | Eine Foto-Vorschau erscheint im Eingabebereich des Chats |
| 4 | 4. Tippe auf Senden | Das Foto wird im Chat gesendet und im Nachrichtenverlauf angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Foto kann aufgenommen, als Vorschau angezeigt und erfolgreich im Chat gesendet werden.

---

### TC-449: Kamera-Berechtigung nicht erteilt – Berechtigungsdialog wird angezeigt

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass bei fehlender Berechtigung ein Dialog angezeigt wird und kein Zugriff erfolgt.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist nicht erteilt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Ein Berechtigungsdialog zur Kamera erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Wähle „Nicht erlauben“ | Die Kamera-Ansicht öffnet sich nicht |

**Final Expected Result:** Ohne Zustimmung wird kein Kamerazugriff ermöglicht.

---

### TC-450: Kamera-Berechtigung erteilen im Dialog

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Stellt sicher, dass nach Zustimmung im Berechtigungsdialog die Kamera geöffnet wird.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist nicht erteilt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Ein Berechtigungsdialog zur Kamera erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Wähle „Erlauben“ | Die Kamera-Ansicht öffnet sich |

**Final Expected Result:** Nach Erteilung der Berechtigung ist der Kamerazugriff möglich.

---

### TC-451: Kamera durch andere App belegt – Fehlermeldung anzeigen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Verifiziert die Fehlermeldung, wenn die Kamera belegt ist.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Kamera ist durch eine andere App belegt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Eine verständliche Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Nutzer erhält eine verständliche Fehlermeldung bei belegter Kamera.

---

### TC-452: Fehlermeldung bei belegter Kamera – Abbrechen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass der Nutzer den Vorgang nach Fehlermeldung abbrechen kann.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Kamera ist durch eine andere App belegt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Eine Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Tippe auf „Abbrechen“ | Die Fehlermeldung schließt sich und der Nutzer bleibt im Chat |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Nutzer kann den Vorgang sauber abbrechen und im Chat bleiben.

---

### TC-453: Fehlermeldung bei belegter Kamera – Erneut versuchen

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Stellt sicher, dass ein erneuter Versuch möglich ist, nachdem die Kamera freigegeben wurde.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Kamera ist initial durch eine andere App belegt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Eine Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Schließe die andere App, die die Kamera belegt | Die Kamera wird freigegeben |
| 3 | 3. Tippe auf „Erneut versuchen“ | Die Kamera-Ansicht öffnet sich |

**Final Expected Result:** Nach Freigabe der Kamera kann der Nutzer erfolgreich erneut versuchen.

---

### TC-454: Fotoaufnahme abbrechen – keine Vorschau im Chat

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass beim Abbruch der Kameraaufnahme keine Vorschau erscheint.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Kamera-Symbol im Chat | Die Kamera-Ansicht öffnet sich |
| 2 | 2. Tippe auf „Abbrechen“ oder Zurück | Die Kamera-Ansicht schließt sich |

**Final Expected Result:** Keine Foto-Vorschau wird im Chat angezeigt.

---

### TC-455: Vorschau löschen vor dem Senden

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass die Foto-Vorschau vor dem Senden entfernt werden kann.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Aktiver Chat ist geöffnet
- Kamera-Berechtigung ist erteilt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Kamera und nimm ein Foto auf | Eine Foto-Vorschau erscheint im Chat |
| 2 | 2. Tippe auf das Löschen/Entfernen-Symbol der Vorschau | Die Foto-Vorschau wird entfernt |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Nutzer kann die Vorschau vor dem Senden entfernen.

---

### TC-456: Kamera-Funktion im inaktiven Chat nicht verfügbar

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-053
**Requirement:** WA-MED-007

**Description:** Boundary-Test: Kamera-Funktion darf nur in aktivem Chat verfügbar sein.

**Preconditions:**
- User ist eingeloggt
- Kein aktiver Chat ist geöffnet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigiere zur Chat-Übersicht ohne aktiven Chat | Kein Kamera-Symbol im Eingabebereich sichtbar |

**Final Expected Result:** Kamera-Funktion ist nur im aktiven Chat verfügbar.

---

### TC-457: Send supported audio file successfully

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify that a supported audio file can be sent and played by recipient

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is logged in
- Recipient is logged in
- Sender is in an active chat with recipient
- Supported audio file (e.g., MP3) exists within size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat with the recipient | Chat thread is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attach the supported audio file | Audio file appears in message composer |
| 3 | 3. Tap the Send button | Send status indicates uploading/sending |
| 4 | 4. Wait for send to complete | Message appears in chat with audio playback control |
| 5 | 5. On recipient side, play the audio file | Audio plays successfully without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Supported audio file is delivered and playable by the recipient

---

### TC-458: Block unsupported audio file type

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify sending is blocked for unsupported audio formats with a clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Unsupported audio file (e.g., .wma) exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an unsupported audio file | System validates the file type |
| 2 | 2. Tap the Send button | Send is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI feedback | A clear error message explains the file type is not supported |

**Final Expected Result:** Unsupported audio file cannot be sent and a meaningful error is displayed

---

### TC-459: Block audio file exceeding maximum size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify sending is blocked when audio file exceeds maximum allowed size

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Audio file exceeds maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an oversized audio file | System detects file size |
| 2 | 2. Tap the Send button | Send is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI feedback | A clear error message indicates the file is too large and shows the size limit |

**Final Expected Result:** Oversized audio file is not sent and a size limit error is shown

---

### TC-460: Boundary test: audio file at maximum size limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify that an audio file exactly at the maximum size limit can be sent

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Audio file equals maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an audio file at the size limit | File is accepted in the composer |
| 2 | 2. Tap the Send button | Send starts successfully |
| 3 | 3. Wait for completion | Message is delivered and audio is playable |

**Final Expected Result:** Audio file at the maximum size limit is sent successfully

---

### TC-461: Boundary test: audio file just over maximum size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify that an audio file 1 byte over the maximum size is blocked

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Audio file is 1 byte over the maximum size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attach an audio file slightly over the limit | System detects size over limit |
| 2 | 2. Tap the Send button | Send is blocked |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI feedback | A clear error message indicates the file exceeds the size limit |

**Final Expected Result:** Audio file just over the limit is not sent and error is displayed

---

### TC-462: Send status and automatic retry on transient network loss

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify send status is shown and transfer retries automatically during unstable network

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Supported audio file within size limit is available
- Network throttling/instability can be simulated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start sending a supported audio file | Send status shows uploading/sending |
| 2 | 2. Simulate network drop during upload | Send status indicates retrying or pending |
| 3 | 3. Restore network connectivity | Upload resumes and completes automatically |
| 4 | 4. Verify delivery in chat | Message appears delivered with playable audio |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows send status and retries automatically to complete transfer after transient network loss

---

### TC-463: Send fails after prolonged network outage

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify transfer is marked failed when network remains unavailable

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Supported audio file within size limit is available
- Network can be disabled for extended period

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start sending a supported audio file | Send status shows uploading/sending |
| 2 | 2. Disable network for a prolonged period | Send status changes to failed after retry attempts/timeouts |
| 3 | 3. Observe UI feedback | Failure indicator and message are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Transfer is marked failed after prolonged outage and user is informed

---

### TC-464: Retry failed send manually after network restored

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-054
**Requirement:** WA-MED-008

**Description:** Verify user can retry sending a failed audio message after network recovery

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in an active chat
- Supported audio file within size limit is available
- A previous send attempt has failed due to network loss

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Restore network connectivity | Device regains network access |
| 2 | 2. Tap the retry option on the failed message | Send restarts and status shows uploading/sending |
| 3 | 3. Wait for completion | Message is delivered with playable audio |

**Final Expected Result:** Failed audio send can be retried successfully after network recovery

---

---

### TC-468: Bild aus Galerie auswählen und im Chat anzeigen

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass ein ausgewähltes Bild aus der Geräte-Galerie im Chat angezeigt wird und gesendet werden kann.

**Preconditions:**
- App ist installiert
- Benutzer ist angemeldet
- Galerie-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Mindestens ein unterstütztes Bild ist in der Galerie vorhanden
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Medienauswahl-Symbol im Chat. | Medienauswahl öffnet sich mit Galerie-Inhalten. |
| 2 | 2. Wähle ein unterstütztes Bild aus der Galerie aus. | Das ausgewählte Bild wird als Vorschau im Chat angezeigt. |
| 3 | 3. Tippe auf den Senden-Button. | Das Bild wird als gesendete Nachricht im Chatverlauf angezeigt. |

**Final Expected Result:** Das Bild wird korrekt ausgewählt, angezeigt und gesendet.

---

### TC-469: Galerie öffnen ohne Berechtigung

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

**Preconditions:**
- App ist installiert
- Benutzer ist angemeldet
- Galerie-Berechtigung ist verweigert
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Medienauswahl-Symbol im Chat. | Eine klare Hinweisnachricht über fehlende Berechtigung erscheint. |
| 2 | 2. Tippe auf die Option zur Berechtigungserteilung. | Systemdialog zur Vergabe der Galerie-Berechtigung wird angezeigt. |

**Final Expected Result:** Der Benutzer wird informiert und kann die Berechtigung erteilen.

---

### TC-470: Berechtigung erteilen und Galerie erneut öffnen

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass nach Erteilung der Berechtigung die Galerieinhalte angezeigt werden.

**Preconditions:**
- App ist installiert
- Benutzer ist angemeldet
- Galerie-Berechtigung ist verweigert
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Tippe auf das Medienauswahl-Symbol und öffne den Berechtigungsdialog. | Hinweisnachricht und Berechtigungsoption werden angezeigt. |
| 2 | 2. Erteile die Galerie-Berechtigung im Systemdialog. | Berechtigung wird erteilt und der Dialog schließt sich. |
| 3 | 3. Öffne erneut die Medienauswahl. | Galerie-Inhalte werden angezeigt. |

**Final Expected Result:** Nach Erteilung der Berechtigung ist die Galerie zugänglich.

---

**Priority:** medium

|---|--------|------------------|

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

### TC-474: Mehrfachauswahl innerhalb der erlaubten Grenze

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

**Description:** Verifiziert, dass mehrere Bilder innerhalb einer zulässigen Grenze ausgewählt und angezeigt werden.

- Benutzer ist angemeldet
- Galerie-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Mehrere unterstützte Bilder sind vorhanden
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet
- Mehrfachauswahl ist in der App erlaubt

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Medienauswahl. | Galerie-Inhalte werden angezeigt. |
| 2 | 2. Wähle mehrere unterstützte Bilder innerhalb der erlaubten Anzahl aus. | Alle ausgewählten Bilder werden als Vorschau im Chat angezeigt. |
| 3 | 3. Tippe auf Senden. | Alle ausgewählten Bilder werden im Chatverlauf gesendet. |

**Final Expected Result:** Mehrfachauswahl funktioniert innerhalb der Grenze.

### TC-475: Mehrfachauswahl über der erlaubten Grenze

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

**Description:** Verifiziert die Grenzbedingung, wenn mehr Medien als erlaubt ausgewählt werden.

**Preconditions:**
- App ist installiert
- Benutzer ist angemeldet
- Galerie-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Mehrere unterstützte Bilder sind vorhanden
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet
- Mehrfachauswahl ist in der App erlaubt mit einer definierten Obergrenze

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Medienauswahl. | Galerie-Inhalte werden angezeigt. |
| 2 | 2. Versuche mehr Bilder als die erlaubte Obergrenze auszuwählen. | Eine Warnung erscheint oder die zusätzliche Auswahl wird verhindert. |

**Final Expected Result:** Die App verhindert Auswahl über der Grenze und bleibt stabil.

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-055
**Requirement:** WA-MED-009

- Galerie-Berechtigung ist erteilt
- Chat-Konversation ist geöffnet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

| 2 | 2. Öffne die Medienauswahl in der App. | Eine Hinweisnachricht über fehlende Berechtigung erscheint. |

**Final Expected Result:** Die App reagiert korrekt auf entzogenes Recht.

### TC-478: Send HD photo successfully with stable connection and supported device

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify HD photo is sent and displayed in HD quality when conditions are met

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Device supports HD media sending
- Stable network connection
- Recipient is reachable
- HD photo within allowed size and resolution is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a chat with a recipient | Chat screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the HD photo and choose 'Send in HD' | HD send option is selected and preview shows HD badge/indicator |
| 3 | 3. Tap send | Upload starts and progress indicator appears |
| 4 | 4. Wait for delivery confirmation | Message status shows sent/delivered |
| 5 | 5. View the received media on recipient device | Photo is displayed in HD quality (resolution matches original HD) |

**Final Expected Result:** HD photo is transmitted and displayed in HD quality on the recipient side

---

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

- Device supports HD media sending
- Stable network connection
- Recipient is reachable
- HD video within allowed size and resolution is available

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a chat with a recipient | Chat screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the HD video and choose 'Send in HD' | HD send option is selected and preview shows HD badge/indicator |
| 3 | 3. Tap send | Upload starts and progress indicator appears |
| 4 | 4. Wait for delivery confirmation | Message status shows sent/delivered |
| 5 | 5. Play the received video on recipient device | Video plays in HD quality (resolution matches original HD) |

**Final Expected Result:** HD video is transmitted and displayed in HD quality on the recipient side

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual

**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

- Device supports HD media sending
- Stable network connection
- HD media file larger than maximum size limit is available

|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a chat with a recipient | Chat screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the oversized HD media file and choose 'Send in HD' | Send action is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to send the media | Sending is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** User sees a clear error message indicating the maximum allowed HD file size limit

**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

### TC-483: Handle unstable network: successful retry completes HD send

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify HD sending completes after temporary network instability

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Device supports HD media sending
- Network is unstable or slow (simulated)
- HD media within allowed limits is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a chat with a recipient | Chat screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the HD media and choose 'Send in HD' | Upload starts and progress indicator appears |
| 3 | 3. Simulate network drop or high latency during upload | App shows retry or resumes upload indicator |
| 4 | 4. Restore network connectivity | Upload resumes automatically or after retry action |
| 5 | 5. Wait for delivery confirmation | Message status shows sent/delivered |

**Final Expected Result:** HD media sending completes reliably after network instability

### TC-484: Handle unstable network: user receives retry message on failure

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify user receives message to retry when HD sending cannot complete

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Device supports HD media sending
- Network is unstable or disconnected (simulated)
- HD media within allowed limits is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a chat with a recipient | Chat screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the HD media and choose 'Send in HD' | Upload starts and progress indicator appears |
| 3 | 3. Simulate prolonged network failure until timeout | Upload fails |
| 4 | 4. Observe the user notification | User is prompted with a clear message to retry sending |

**Final Expected Result:** User receives a clear retry message when HD sending fails due to unstable network

| 2 | 2. Select the HD media and choose 'Send in HD' | Upload starts and progress indicator appears |
| 3 | 3. Measure time from send action to delivery confirmation | Total send time is recorded |

**Final Expected Result:** HD media is delivered within the defined performance threshold for stable network conditions

### TC-486: E2E encryption on send and receive between two registered devices

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify that a sent message is end-to-end encrypted in transit and only decrypted on recipient device

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient are registered users
- Both users are logged in on their respective devices
- A conversation between the users is open on both devices

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender types a message with alphanumeric and special characters | Message is composed and visible in sender input field |
| 2 | 2. Sender taps the Send button | Message is sent and appears in sender chat with a sent status |
| 3 | 3. Observe network payload or transport logs for the sent message | Payload is ciphertext and contains no readable plaintext |
| 4 | 4. Recipient receives the message | Message is displayed in plaintext on recipient device only |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is encrypted during transport/storage and decrypted only on recipient device

---

### TC-487: Unauthorized access cannot read stored encrypted message

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify stored messages are unreadable without device keys

**Preconditions:**
- At least one message has been sent between two registered users
- Database or message store can be accessed by a system service account without device keys

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Access stored message records using a system service account | Message content is stored as ciphertext |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to interpret or decode the stored message content without device keys | Content remains unreadable and cannot be decrypted |

**Final Expected Result:** Stored messages remain unreadable without end-device keys

---

### TC-488: Unauthorized access cannot read message in transit

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify intercepted transport traffic is unreadable without device keys

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient are registered users
- Both users are logged in and conversation is open
- Network inspection tool is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start network capture on the transport channel | Capture begins and traffic is recorded |
| 2 | 2. Send a message from sender to recipient | Message is sent successfully |
| 3 | 3. Inspect captured payload for the message content | Payload is encrypted and contains no readable plaintext |

**Final Expected Result:** In-transit messages are unreadable without end-device keys

---

### TC-489: Receive message with missing or invalid recipient key

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify message is not shown in plaintext and user receives recovery error

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient device key is missing or invalid
- Sender is registered and logged in
- Conversation is open on sender

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender sends a message to the recipient | Message is sent successfully |
| 2 | 2. Recipient attempts to receive the message | Message is not displayed in plaintext |
| 3 | 3. Observe recipient UI notification | Error message appears with recovery guidance |

**Final Expected Result:** Recipient cannot decrypt and is informed with recovery instructions

---

### TC-490: Receive message with inconsistent key state between devices

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify handling when recipient device key differs from conversation key

**Preconditions:**
- Recipient has multiple devices; one device has outdated or inconsistent keys
- Sender and recipient are registered and logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Sender sends a message to recipient | Message is sent successfully |
| 2 | 2. Recipient opens conversation on device with inconsistent keys | Message is not shown in plaintext |
| 3 | 3. Observe recipient UI notification | Error message indicates key inconsistency and recovery steps |

**Final Expected Result:** Message remains encrypted and user is prompted to recover keys

---

### TC-491: Boundary: Send empty or whitespace-only message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify system behavior for boundary input while maintaining encryption requirements

**Preconditions:**
- Sender is registered and logged in
- Conversation is open

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an empty or whitespace-only message | Input is recognized as empty/invalid |
| 2 | 2. Tap Send | Message is not sent and user is prompted to enter valid content |

**Final Expected Result:** No message is transmitted; encryption workflow is not triggered for invalid input

---

### TC-492: Boundary: Maximum allowed message length encryption and delivery

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify encryption and delivery for maximum-sized message

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient are registered users
- Conversation is open
- Maximum message length is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Compose a message exactly at maximum allowed length | Message is accepted in the input field |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is sent successfully and appears as sent |
| 3 | 3. Recipient receives the message | Message is displayed correctly in plaintext |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum-length message is encrypted in transit and decrypted on recipient device without truncation

---

### TC-493: Negative: Attempt to decrypt with wrong device keys

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify that using incorrect keys cannot decrypt message content

**Preconditions:**
- Encrypted message exists in transport or storage
- A device with non-matching keys is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt decryption of the encrypted payload using incorrect keys | Decryption fails |
| 2 | 2. Observe any output or logs | No plaintext content is produced |

**Final Expected Result:** Message remains unreadable without correct end-device keys

---

### TC-494: Successful manual verification of matching security code

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify that matching codes can be confirmed and shown as verified

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a displayed security code
- Communication partner has the same security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the conversation with the displayed security code | Security code is visible for the conversation |
| 2 | 2. Initiate manual verification from the security code UI | System prompts user to confirm the code matches the partner's code |
| 3 | 3. Confirm that the code matches and mark it as verified | Verification process completes without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows verification status as successful for the conversation

---

### TC-495: Re-verification required after security code change

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify that a changed security code triggers a re-verification request

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation previously verified
- Security code has changed for the conversation

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the conversation where the security code has changed | System indicates the code has changed or verification is no longer valid |
| 2 | 2. Start the verification flow again | System prompts for manual confirmation of the new security code |

**Final Expected Result:** User is required to manually confirm the new security code before verification can be marked successful

---

### TC-496: Failed verification for mismatched security code

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify warning and failure status when codes do not match

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a displayed security code
- Communication partner has a different security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the conversation with the displayed security code | Security code is visible for the conversation |
| 2 | 2. Initiate manual verification | System prompts user to confirm the code matches the partner's code |
| 3 | 3. Indicate that the code does not match and complete verification | System processes the verification outcome |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows a warning and marks verification as failed

---

### TC-497: Boundary: Verification immediately after code change without user confirmation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure user cannot be marked verified without confirming the new code

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a recently changed security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the conversation with the changed security code | Verification status indicates re-verification required |
| 2 | 2. Navigate away from the verification prompt without confirming | Verification remains pending or unverified |

**Final Expected Result:** System does not show verification as successful until user confirms the new code

---

### TC-498: Negative: Attempt to verify without partner comparison

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure the system requires explicit user confirmation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a displayed security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the conversation and open verification flow | Verification prompt appears |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to finalize verification without confirming match (e.g., close prompt or submit without selection) | System prevents completion and keeps status unverified |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification cannot be completed without explicit confirmation of code match

---

### TC-499: Integration: Verification status persists after refresh

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify that successful verification status is stored and displayed after reload

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a matching security code
- User has successfully verified the security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Refresh the conversation view or re-open the app | Conversation loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Open the same conversation | Security code and verification status are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** System still shows the verification status as successful

---

### TC-500: Negative: Verification fails when code changes mid-verification

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure the system handles a code change during verification and requires re-confirmation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An existing conversation with a displayed security code
- Security code can change due to key update or device change

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start verification and view the current security code | Verification prompt is active with current code |
| 2 | 2. Trigger a code change (e.g., partner rekeys) before confirming | System detects the code change |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to confirm the old code as matching | System blocks confirmation and prompts for new code verification |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification is not marked successful; user must confirm the new code

---

### TC-501: Enable app lock with authentication method

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that enabling app lock and selecting an authentication method locks the app and requires authentication on every open

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- User is logged in
- Device supports authentication (PIN/biometric)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Security | Security settings screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Toggle 'App Lock' to ON | Prompt to select an authentication method appears |
| 3 | 3. Select 'Device PIN' and complete setup | App lock is enabled and confirmation is shown |
| 4 | 4. Close the app completely | App is closed |
| 5 | 5. Reopen the app | Authentication prompt is shown before any messages are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** App remains locked on open and requires successful authentication to access messages

---

### TC-502: Unlock app with correct authentication

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify successful authentication unlocks the app and grants access to messages

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter the correct authentication (PIN/biometric) | Authentication succeeds |
| 3 | 3. Observe the app state after authentication | Messages screen is displayed and content is visible |

**Final Expected Result:** App unlocks and user has access to messages

---

### TC-503: Deny access on incorrect authentication

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that incorrect authentication prevents access and keeps messages hidden

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter an incorrect PIN or fail biometric authentication | Authentication fails and an error message is shown |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to view messages without authenticating | Messages remain hidden and access is denied |

**Final Expected Result:** Access is denied after failed authentication and messages remain protected

---

### TC-504: App remains locked after backgrounding and reopening

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that the app requires authentication when reopened from background

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app and authenticate successfully | App is unlocked and messages are visible |
| 2 | 2. Send the app to background for 30 seconds | App is running in background |
| 3 | 3. Return to the app | Authentication prompt is displayed before messages |

**Final Expected Result:** App requires authentication on reopen and does not expose messages

---

### TC-505: Cancel authentication attempt

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that canceling authentication keeps the app locked and messages hidden

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Cancel' or dismiss the authentication prompt | Authentication is not completed and app remains locked |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to navigate to messages | Access is blocked and messages are not visible |

**Final Expected Result:** App stays locked when authentication is canceled

---

### TC-506: Multiple failed authentication attempts

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify behavior after consecutive failed authentication attempts

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter an incorrect authentication three times consecutively | Each attempt fails with an error message |
| 3 | 3. Observe any lockout or cooldown indication | If lockout is implemented, user is informed and access remains denied |

**Final Expected Result:** App continues to deny access after multiple failed attempts and messages remain hidden

---

### TC-507: Switch authentication method

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that changing authentication method still enforces app lock on open

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Security | Security settings screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Change authentication method from PIN to biometric | New method is saved and confirmed |
| 3 | 3. Close and reopen the app | Authentication prompt uses the newly selected method |

**Final Expected Result:** App lock remains enforced with the updated authentication method

---

### TC-508: No message preview on lock screen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that messages are not visible before authentication at app launch

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed immediately |
| 2 | 2. Observe the screen behind the authentication prompt | No message content is visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Messages and sensitive data are not displayed before authentication

---

### TC-509: App lock persists after device restart

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that app lock remains enabled after device reboot

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Restart the device | Device restarts successfully |
| 2 | 2. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Authenticate successfully | Messages are accessible |

**Final Expected Result:** App lock remains active across device restart

---

### TC-510: Attempt access via app switcher preview

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that messages are not exposed in app switcher preview when app is locked

**Preconditions:**
- App lock is enabled
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app and authenticate | Messages are visible |
| 2 | 2. Send the app to background | App is in background |
| 3 | 3. Open the app switcher | App preview does not show message content or is obfuscated |

**Final Expected Result:** Messages are protected in app switcher when app lock is enabled

---

### TC-511: Block existing contact successfully

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can block an existing contact and the contact can no longer message

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A has existing contact User B
- User B is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact details for User B | Contact details page for User B is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Blockieren" option | "Blockieren" confirmation dialog is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the block action | User B is marked as blocked and success message is shown |
| 4 | 4. From User B account, attempt to send a message to User A | Message is not delivered to User A |

**Final Expected Result:** User B is blocked and cannot send messages to User A

---

### TC-512: Blocked contact receives feedback when sending message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify blocked contact receives an appropriate feedback when message delivery is prevented

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A has blocked User B

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. From User B account, compose a message to User A | Message composition screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | System rejects the message |
| 3 | 3. Observe sender feedback | User B receives a clear notification that the message was not delivered due to being blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocked sender is informed that the message was not delivered

---

### TC-513: Block non-existent contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify blocking a non-existent contact results in an error and no changes

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to block a contact with an invalid or non-existent contact ID | Block request is submitted |
| 2 | 2. Observe system response | Error message is displayed indicating contact does not exist |
| 3 | 3. Check blocked contacts list | No new blocked contact is added |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocking fails with an error and no data changes occur

---

### TC-514: Block action cancelled by user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify cancellation of blocking does not block the contact

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A has existing contact User B
- User B is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact details for User B | Contact details page for User B is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Blockieren" option | "Blockieren" confirmation dialog is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Cancel the block action | Dialog closes and no success message is shown |
| 4 | 4. From User B account, send a message to User A | Message is delivered to User A |

**Final Expected Result:** User B remains unblocked and can message User A

---

### TC-515: Block contact already blocked

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify attempting to block an already blocked contact results in appropriate feedback without changes

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A has blocked User B

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact details for User B | Contact details page for User B is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the "Blockieren" option | System indicates User B is already blocked or block action is disabled |

**Final Expected Result:** No duplicate block is created and user receives appropriate feedback

---

### TC-516: Blocked contact cannot deliver message after block is applied

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify messages sent immediately after blocking are not delivered (boundary timing case)

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User A has existing contact User B
- User B is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A blocks User B and confirms | User B is marked as blocked |
| 2 | 2. Immediately from User B, send a message to User A | Message is rejected and not delivered |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocking takes effect immediately and messages are not delivered

---

### TC-517: Report message successfully with valid reason

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify that a user can report a received message with a selected reason and receives confirmation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least one received message in a chat
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the received message | The chat view displays the received message |
| 2 | 2. Select the message options menu | The message options are displayed, including 'Melden' |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Melden' | The report dialog/screen opens with a list of report reasons |
| 4 | 4. Select a valid report reason | The selected reason is highlighted/selected |
| 5 | 5. Tap 'Senden' or 'Bestätigen' | The report is submitted |

**Final Expected Result:** The report is successfully saved and a confirmation message is shown to the user

---

### TC-518: Report contact successfully with valid reason

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify that a user can report a contact with a selected reason and receives confirmation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least one contact or chat partner
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact/profile view from a chat | The contact/profile view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap the options menu and select 'Melden' | The report dialog/screen opens with a list of report reasons |
| 3 | 3. Select a valid report reason | The selected reason is highlighted/selected |
| 4 | 4. Tap 'Senden' or 'Bestätigen' | The report is submitted |

**Final Expected Result:** The report is successfully saved and a confirmation message is shown to the user

---

### TC-519: Prevent report submission without selecting a reason

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify that the report cannot be sent without selecting a reason

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing a received message or contact
- Network connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the report dialog by selecting 'Melden' | The report dialog/screen opens with a list of report reasons |
| 2 | 2. Do not select any reason | No reason is selected |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Senden' or 'Bestätigen' | Submission is blocked and an error/prompt is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** The report is not sent and the user is prompted to select a reason

---

### TC-520: Handle network interruption during report submission

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify that the user receives an error when reporting without network connection

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing a received message or contact
- Network connection is unavailable
