### TC-709: Admin sets permission to only administrators can add members

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that an administrator can update group invitation permission to 'Only administrators can add'

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- User is logged in as group administrator

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group settings page | Group settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Locate the invitation permission setting | Current permission setting is visible |
| 3 | 3. Select the option 'Only administrators can add' | Option is selected and pending save |
| 4 | 4. Save changes | Success message is displayed and setting is updated |

**Final Expected Result:** Group invitation permission is set to 'Only administrators can add'

---

### TC-710: Admin can add a member when only admins can add

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that admins can add members under restricted permission

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'Only administrators can add'
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Target user exists and is not a member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Add member' and enter target user identifier | Target user is found or validated |
| 3 | 3. Confirm adding the member | Success message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Target user is added to the group and appears in the member list

---

### TC-711: Non-admin cannot add member when only admins can add

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that a normal member is prevented from adding when restricted

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'Only administrators can add'
- User is logged in as normal group member
- Target user exists and is not a member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to click 'Add member' and enter target user identifier | Add member action is blocked or proceeds to validation |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to confirm adding the member | Operation is rejected with a clear error message |

**Final Expected Result:** Member is not added; a clear error message indicates insufficient permissions

---

### TC-712: Permission error message clarity for non-admin add attempt

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify error message content and visibility for unauthorized add attempts

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'Only administrators can add'
- User is logged in as normal group member
- Target user exists and is not a member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to add a new member from members page | Operation is rejected |
| 2 | 2. Observe the error message | Error message is visible, clear, and indicates only admins can add members |

**Final Expected Result:** Error message is clearly displayed and no member is added

---

### TC-713: Non-admin can add member when all members can add

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that a normal member can add members when permission allows

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'All members can add'
- User is logged in as normal group member
- Target user exists and is not a member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Add member' and enter target user identifier | Target user is found or validated |
| 3 | 3. Confirm adding the member | Success message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Target user is added to the group and appears in the member list

---

### TC-714: Toggle permission from only admins to all members

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that changing permission allows members to add afterwards

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Group invitation permission is set to 'Only administrators can add'
- Normal member user exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Change group invitation permission to 'All members can add' and save | Setting is updated successfully |
| 2 | 2. Log in as normal member | Normal member is logged in |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to add a target user | Add member operation succeeds |

**Final Expected Result:** Normal member can add new members after permission change

---

### TC-715: Attempt to add an already existing member

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify system response when adding a user who is already a member

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'All members can add'
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Target user is already a group member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to add an existing member | System detects duplication |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the add action | Operation is rejected with a clear message |

**Final Expected Result:** No duplicate membership is created; a clear error message is shown

---

### TC-716: Unauthorized user cannot change invitation permission

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify that non-admin cannot edit invitation permission setting

- Group exists
- User is logged in as normal group member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to group settings page | Settings page is displayed or access is restricted |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to change invitation permission | Change controls are disabled or access is denied |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to save changes | Operation is blocked with a permission error |

**Final Expected Result:** Non-admin cannot modify invitation permission and receives a clear error

---

### TC-717: Boundary: Add member with minimal valid identifier

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify adding a member using the shortest valid identifier

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'All members can add'
- User is logged in as group administrator
- Target user with minimal valid identifier exists and is not a member

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |

| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter the shortest valid user identifier and submit | Identifier is accepted |

---

### TC-718: Boundary: Add member with invalid/empty identifier

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-084
**Requirement:** WA-SET-005

**Description:** Verify validation when identifier is empty or invalid

**Preconditions:**
- Group exists
- Group invitation permission is set to 'All members can add'
- User is logged in as group administrator

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the group members page | Members page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Add member' and leave identifier empty or enter invalid format | Validation message is shown |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to confirm adding the member | Operation is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** No member is added and a validation error is shown

### TC-719: Display storage overview for authorized admin

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify overview shows total, per-area usage, and remaining storage for authorized admin

**Preconditions:**
- System administrator account exists
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- Storage usage data is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads without errors |
| 2 | 2. Observe the summary section | Total consumption, usage per area, and remaining storage are displayed clearly |

**Final Expected Result:** Storage overview displays total usage, per-area usage, and remaining storage for authorized admin

---

### TC-720: Access denied for unauthorized user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Ensure users without storage management permission cannot access storage overview

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User does not have permission for storage management

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the storage usage overview page | Access is denied or user is redirected with an authorization error |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthorized user cannot view storage usage overview

---

### TC-721: Filter by area with high storage consumption

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify filtering by area updates values correctly and recalculates totals

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- Multiple areas have stored data with high storage consumption

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads with default totals |
| 2 | 2. Apply a filter for a specific area | Filtered usage values for that area are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Check the total consumption in the overview | Total consumption updates to reflect the filtered area data only |

**Final Expected Result:** Filtering by area shows correct per-area values and updated total

---

### TC-722: Filter by time period with data

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify filtering by time period updates values correctly and recalculates totals

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- Storage usage data exists across multiple time periods

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads with default totals |
| 2 | 2. Apply a time period filter (e.g., last 30 days) | Usage values are updated to reflect only the selected time period |
| 3 | 3. Verify the total consumption value | Total consumption updates to match the filtered time period |

**Final Expected Result:** Filtering by time period shows correct values and updated total

---

### TC-723: Filter with no matching data (boundary)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Ensure filters with no matching data show zero values without errors

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- There exists at least one area with no data for a specific time period

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Apply filters for an area and time period with no data | Filtered view displays zeros for usage values |
| 3 | 3. Check the total consumption | Total consumption is 0 and no error is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** No-data filters show zero values and no errors

---

### TC-724: Detail view for area with no stored data

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify that detail view shows 0 usage and no errors for empty area

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- An area exists with no stored data

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the detail view of an area with no stored data | Detail view loads without errors |
| 3 | 3. Observe the usage value for the area | Usage is displayed as 0 |

**Final Expected Result:** Detail view shows 0 usage and no errors for empty area

---

### TC-725: Storage data temporarily unavailable error message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify clear error message is shown when storage data is unavailable

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- Storage usage data service is temporarily unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the storage usage overview page | Page attempts to load data and fails |
| 2 | 2. Observe the error message | A clear error message is displayed with guidance to retry later |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows clear error message with retry guidance when data is unavailable

---

### TC-726: Combined filters by area and time period

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify applying both area and time period filters yields correct data and totals

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- Storage usage data exists across multiple areas and time periods

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the storage usage overview page | Overview page loads with default totals |
| 2 | 2. Apply an area filter | Usage values update to the selected area |
| 3 | 3. Apply a time period filter | Usage values update to the selected area and time period |
| 4 | 4. Verify total consumption | Total matches the combined filters |

**Final Expected Result:** Combined filters show correct usage values and updated total

---

### TC-727: High-volume data rendering performance

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-085
**Requirement:** WA-SET-006

**Description:** Verify overview renders within acceptable time for high storage volumes

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has permission for storage management
- System contains high-volume storage data across many areas

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the storage usage overview page | Page starts loading data |
| 2 | 2. Measure time until the overview is fully rendered | Overview renders within the defined performance threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** Overview loads within acceptable performance limits under high data volume

---

### TC-728: Datennutzung anzeigen bei aktiver Erfassung

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass aktuelle und historische Datennutzung pro Zeitraum angezeigt wird, wenn die Erfassung aktiv ist.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Es existieren aktuelle und historische Datennutzungsdaten

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne das Hauptmenü. | Hauptmenü wird angezeigt. |
| 2 | 2. Wähle die Ansicht „Datennutzung“. | Die Ansicht „Datennutzung“ wird geöffnet. |
| 3 | 3. Prüfe die Anzeige des aktuellen Datenverbrauchs. | Der aktuelle Datenverbrauch wird mit Einheiten (z. B. MB/GB) angezeigt. |
| 4 | 4. Prüfe die Anzeige historischer Verbräuche pro Zeitraum. | Historische Datenverbrauchswerte werden pro Zeitraum (z. B. Tag/Woche/Monat) angezeigt. |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Ansicht zeigt korrekt den aktuellen und historischen Datenverbrauch pro Zeitraum an.

---

### TC-729: Datennutzungslimit erreicht – Nutzerbenachrichtigung und Reduktion

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass bei Erreichen/Überschreiten des Limits informiert und Datennutzung reduziert wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Ein Datenlimit ist definiert
- Die Reduktionsoption ist konfiguriert (z. B. reduzierte Bildqualität)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simuliere Datenverbrauch bis exakt zum definierten Limit. | Der Datenverbrauch erreicht den Grenzwert. |
| 2 | 2. Beobachte die Systemreaktion. | Der Nutzer wird über das Erreichen des Limits informiert. |
| 3 | 3. Simuliere zusätzlichen Datenverbrauch über das Limit hinaus. | Das System reduziert die Datennutzung gemäß gewählter Einstellung. |

**Final Expected Result:** Bei Erreichen/Überschreiten des Limits erfolgt eine Benachrichtigung und eine Reduktion der Datennutzung.

---

### TC-730: Datennutzungslimit überschritten – Reduktion greift weiterhin

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Negativ-/Grenzfall: Prüft Verhalten bei Überschreiten des Limits.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Ein Datenlimit ist definiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simuliere Datenverbrauch, der das Limit um 1% überschreitet. | Der Datenverbrauch liegt über dem Grenzwert. |
| 2 | 2. Öffne die Ansicht „Datennutzung“. | Der Nutzer sieht die Überschreitung und eine aktive Reduktionsmaßnahme. |

**Final Expected Result:** Das System informiert und hält die Reduktion der Datennutzung aufrecht, wenn das Limit überschritten ist.

---

### TC-731: Kein Datenlimit gesetzt – keine Benachrichtigung/Reduktion

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Negativfall: Prüft, dass ohne Limit keine Reduktionslogik greift.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Kein Datenlimit ist definiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simuliere kontinuierlichen Datenverbrauch. | Der Datenverbrauch steigt ohne Limitüberschreitung. |
| 2 | 2. Beobachte Benachrichtigungen und Einstellungen. | Es erfolgt keine Limit-Benachrichtigung und keine Reduktionsmaßnahme. |

**Final Expected Result:** Ohne gesetztes Limit werden keine Limit-bezogenen Aktionen ausgelöst.

---

### TC-732: Datennutzungserfassung nicht verfügbar – Fehlermeldung mit Retry

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Prüft, dass bei Nichtverfügbarkeit eine verständliche Fehlermeldung und Retry angezeigt wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist nicht verfügbar

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Ansicht „Datennutzung“. | Eine verständliche Fehlermeldung wird angezeigt. |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die angebotene erneute Versuchsmöglichkeit (Retry). | Ein Retry-Button oder Link ist sichtbar. |
| 3 | 3. Klicke auf Retry. | Das System versucht erneut, die Datennutzung zu laden. |

**Final Expected Result:** Bei Nichtverfügbarkeit erscheint eine verständliche Fehlermeldung mit erneuter Versuchsmöglichkeit.

---

### TC-733: Historische Datenverbrauchszeiträume korrekt gruppiert

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Prüft die korrekte Gruppierung historischer Daten nach Zeitraum.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Historische Daten für mehrere Zeiträume vorhanden

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Ansicht „Datennutzung“. | Die Ansicht wird geladen. |
| 2 | 2. Wechsle zwischen den Zeitraum-Filtern (z. B. Tag/Woche/Monat). | Die angezeigten historischen Werte wechseln entsprechend dem gewählten Zeitraum. |

**Final Expected Result:** Historische Daten werden korrekt nach ausgewähltem Zeitraum angezeigt.

---

### TC-734: Grenzwert bei exakt 0 und Minimalverbrauch

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Grenzfall: Prüft, dass 0-Verbrauch korrekt dargestellt wird.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Aktueller Datenverbrauch ist 0

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Ansicht „Datennutzung“. | Die Ansicht wird geladen. |
| 2 | 2. Prüfe die Anzeige des aktuellen Verbrauchs. | Der aktuelle Verbrauch wird als 0 mit korrekter Einheit angezeigt. |

**Final Expected Result:** Ein Verbrauch von 0 wird korrekt und ohne Fehler dargestellt.

---

### TC-735: Performance: Datennutzung Ansicht lädt innerhalb akzeptabler Zeit

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-086
**Requirement:** WA-SET-007

**Description:** Prüft die Ladezeit der Datennutzungsansicht bei aktiver Erfassung.

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist angemeldet
- Datennutzungserfassung ist aktiv
- Historische Daten sind vorhanden

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Öffne die Ansicht „Datennutzung“ und starte die Zeitmessung. | Zeitmessung beginnt beim Öffnen. |
| 2 | 2. Warte bis alle aktuellen und historischen Daten geladen sind. | Alle Daten werden vollständig angezeigt. |
| 3 | 3. Stoppe die Zeitmessung. | Die Ladezeit liegt innerhalb des definierten Performance-Ziels. |

**Final Expected Result:** Die Ansicht „Datennutzung“ lädt performant und erfüllt die Ladezeitvorgaben.

---

### TC-736: Select and save predefined chat background

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Verify that selecting a predefined background and saving applies it and persists across sessions.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in chat view
- Predefined backgrounds are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background customization panel in chat view | Background selection options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a predefined background | Selected background is highlighted/previewed |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Save' or 'Apply' | Success confirmation is shown (if applicable) and chat background updates |
| 4 | 4. Log out and log back in, then open the same chat | Previously selected background is still applied |

**Final Expected Result:** Selected background is applied and persists across sessions.

---

### TC-737: Cancel background selection without saving

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Ensure background remains unchanged when selection is not saved.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in chat view
- A current background is already set

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background customization panel | Background options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a different predefined background | New background is highlighted/previewed |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Cancel' or close the panel without saving | Panel closes and no save confirmation appears |

**Final Expected Result:** The original background remains unchanged.

---

### TC-738: Upload valid custom background file

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Verify that a supported file format and size uploads successfully and becomes the chat background.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in chat view
- Upload option is available
- Valid image file in supported format and within size limit is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background customization panel and choose 'Upload' | File picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid image file | Upload begins or file is queued |
| 3 | 3. Confirm upload/apply | Upload completes without error and background updates to uploaded image |

**Final Expected Result:** Custom background is accepted and displayed in the chat.

---

### TC-739: Upload file with unsupported format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Verify proper error handling for unsupported file formats.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in chat view
- Upload option is available
- File with unsupported format is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background customization panel and choose 'Upload' | File picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select an unsupported file format | Upload attempt is rejected or processed |
| 3 | 3. Observe system response | User sees a clear error message about unsupported format |

**Final Expected Result:** Error message is shown and current background remains unchanged.

---

### TC-740: Upload file exceeding size limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Ensure proper error message and no background change for oversized files.

- User is in chat view
- Upload option is available
- File larger than allowed size is available

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background customization panel and choose 'Upload' | File picker opens |
| 2 | 2. Select an oversized image file | Upload attempt is rejected or processed |
| 3 | 3. Observe system response | User sees a clear error message about file size limit |

**Final Expected Result:** Error message is shown and current background remains unchanged.

---

### TC-741: Boundary: Upload file at maximum allowed size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Validate that a file exactly at the size limit is accepted.

- Upload option is available
- File exactly at max size and in supported format is available

| 2 | 2. Select the max-size supported image file | Upload begins or file is queued |
| 3 | 3. Confirm upload/apply | Upload completes successfully and background updates |

**Final Expected Result:** File at size limit is accepted and displayed.

---

### TC-742: Boundary: Upload file just over maximum size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Validate that a file just above size limit is rejected.

- User is in chat view
- Upload option is available
- File slightly larger than max size and in supported format is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

| 2 | 2. Select the oversized image file | Upload attempt is rejected or processed |
| 3 | 3. Observe system response | User sees an error message indicating the size limit |

**Final Expected Result:** Oversized file is rejected and background remains unchanged.

---

### TC-743: Persistence of custom uploaded background across sessions

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

| # | Action | Expected Result |

| 2 | 2. Log back in and open the same chat | Chat view loads |

**Final Expected Result:** Custom uploaded background remains applied.

### TC-744: Background remains unchanged after failed upload

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Ensure current background is preserved after an invalid upload attempt.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in chat view
- A background is already set
- An invalid upload file is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to upload an invalid file (unsupported format or oversized) | System processes the upload attempt |
| 2 | 2. Observe the background and error message | Error message is shown and background does not change |

**Final Expected Result:** Current background remains unchanged after failed upload.

### TC-746: Activate Dark Mode switches UI to dark theme immediately

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that enabling Dark Mode applies the dark color scheme across the entire UI instantly.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Settings screen
- Dark Mode is currently disabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Toggle the Dark Mode switch to ON | The toggle changes to ON state and no errors are shown |
| 2 | 2. Observe the Settings screen and navigate to another main screen (e.g., Home) | All visible UI elements switch to dark theme immediately on both screens |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark theme is applied instantly across the entire app without visual glitches.

---

### TC-747: Dark Mode persists after app restart

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that Dark Mode remains enabled after closing and reopening the app.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Dark Mode is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Close the app completely | App is no longer running |
| 2 | 2. Reopen the app | App launches successfully |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to any main screen and Settings | UI remains in dark theme and Dark Mode toggle is ON |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark Mode setting is persisted across app restarts.

---

### TC-748: Deactivate Dark Mode switches UI back to light theme

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that disabling Dark Mode returns the UI to light theme without display issues.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Settings screen
- Dark Mode is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Toggle the Dark Mode switch to OFF | The toggle changes to OFF state and no errors are shown |
| 2 | 2. Observe the Settings screen and navigate to another main screen (e.g., Home) | All visible UI elements switch back to light theme immediately |

**Final Expected Result:** Light theme is applied across the app with no rendering artifacts.

---

### TC-749: Dark Mode toggle is available only when logged in

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify access control: Dark Mode setting should not be available when user is not logged in.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged out

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app and navigate to Settings (if accessible) | Settings screen is not accessible or requires login |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to access Dark Mode toggle | Dark Mode toggle is not visible or prompts login |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark Mode setting is accessible only for logged-in users.

---

### TC-750: Rapid toggle of Dark Mode does not cause UI corruption

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify stability when toggling Dark Mode quickly multiple times.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on Settings screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Toggle Dark Mode ON and OFF rapidly five times | Toggle responds each time without freezing |
| 2 | 2. Stop on ON state and navigate to another screen | UI renders in dark theme correctly |
| 3 | 3. Return to Settings and toggle OFF | UI switches back to light theme correctly |

**Final Expected Result:** No UI glitches, crashes, or inconsistent theme state occur during rapid toggling.

---

### TC-751: Theme applied to all key UI components in Dark Mode

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that primary UI elements adopt dark theme colors.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Dark Mode is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate through main screens (Home, Profile, Settings, and a detail page) | Backgrounds, text, buttons, and icons use dark theme styling on each screen |
| 2 | 2. Open a modal or dialog (if available) | Modal uses dark theme colors and readable contrast |

**Final Expected Result:** All major UI components follow the dark theme styling consistently.

---

### TC-752: Theme persistence after logout and login

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that Dark Mode setting persists for the user after logout and login.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Dark Mode is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out of the app | User is logged out and returns to login screen |
| 2 | 2. Log back in with the same user | Login is successful |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to a main screen and Settings | Dark Mode is still enabled and UI is dark |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark Mode preference persists across logout/login for the same user.

---

### TC-753: Dark Mode toggle is saved when app is backgrounded

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that the theme remains after backgrounding and resuming the app.

- User is logged in
- Dark Mode is enabled

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

| 2 | 2. Resume the app | App resumes successfully |

### TC-754: Invalid theme state fallback

**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

**Final Expected Result:** App defaults to a safe theme state without errors or unreadable UI.

### TC-756: Language selection persists after reload

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual

### TC-757: Language selection persists after closing and reopening

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that the selected language is retained after closing and reopening the app
